-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `academic_events`
--

CREATE TABLE `academic_events` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `type` enum('exam','project','assignment','quiz','lab','mst','other') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'other',
  `due_date` datetime NOT NULL,
  `description` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `priority` enum('low','medium','high') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
  `subject_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `college_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `course_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `semester` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_official` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `is_verified` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `verification_count` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `academic_events`
--

INSERT INTO `academic_events` (`id`, `title`, `type`, `due_date`, `description`, `priority`, `subject_id`, `college_id`, `course_id`, `semester`, `user_id`, `is_official`, `is_verified`, `verification_count`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(1, 'MST1 Submission', 'assignment', '2026-04-18 00:00:00', NULL, 'medium', 3, 5, NULL, NULL, 5, 0, 0, 0, '2026-04-17 17:59:18', '2026-04-17 17:59:18');

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `academic_guides`
--

CREATE TABLE `academic_guides` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `content` longtext NOT NULL,
  `file_path` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `category` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `meta_title` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `meta_description` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `meta_keywords` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `target_university` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `target_course` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `featured_image` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_published` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  `views` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `academic_guides`
--

INSERT INTO `academic_guides` (`id`, `user_id`, `title`, `slug`, `content`, `file_path`, `category`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `meta_keywords`, `target_university`, `target_course`, `featured_image`, `is_published`, `views`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(2, 5, 'Best B.Sc. IT Colleges in Mumbai with 70% Marks in 12th [2025 Update]', 'best-bsc-it-colleges-in-mumbai-with-70-marks-in-12th-2025-update-pWtJF', '<p>With a <strong>70% in PCM</strong>, your strategy for Mumbai colleges changes slightly. While the absolute \"top-tier\" colleges like St. Xavier’s or Mithibai often close their first lists in the 85%–90% range, Mumbai University\'s admission process happens in <strong>three merit lists</strong>.</p><p><br></p><p>At 70%, you are in a strong position for several reputable \"A-Grade\" colleges, especially in the second and third rounds or through the Minority Quota if applicable to you.</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\"></p><p><br></p><p><br></p><h3><strong>Category Wise Breakdown (Tailored to 70%)</strong></h3><h4><strong>1. Best Academic Value: Bhavan’s College (Andheri)</strong></h4><p>Bhavan’s is a massive, lush green campus. It is highly respected for its Science faculty. At 70%, you have a very high chance of getting into B.Sc. CS here. The environment is academic-focused but offers a great \"college feel\" with its large open spaces.</p><p><br></p><h4><strong>2. Best Placement Potential: Vidyalankar (VSIT) or UPG</strong></h4><ul><li><strong>VSIT (Wadala):</strong> This is a tech-heavy campus. Because they also have an Engineering college on the same premises, many IT mass recruiters (TCS, LTI, Infosys) visit the campus. They are very proactive with B.Sc. IT students.</li><li><strong>UPG (Vile Parle):</strong> Part of the SVKM group (same as Mithibai). While Mithibai\'s cutoff is higher, UPG often takes students in the 70s range and shares some of the same corporate networking benefits.</li></ul><p><br></p><h4><strong>3. Best Campus Life: Pillai College (HOCL/Panvel)</strong></h4><p>If you don\'t mind a bit of a commute (or staying in a hostel), Pillai has arguably the best campus culture in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. Their festival \"Alegria\" is world-class. Academically, their IT department is very well-equipped with modern labs.</p><p><br></p><h3><strong>\"Pro-Tips\" for your 70% Score</strong></h3><ul><li><strong>The \"Maths\" Weightage:</strong> Mumbai University often calculates the merit list for B.Sc. IT specifically based on your <strong>Marks in Mathematics</strong>, not just the overall percentage. If your Math score is 75+, your chances at colleges like KC or Jai Hind increase significantly even with a 70% aggregate.</li><li><strong>Minority Quota:</strong> Mumbai has many linguistic minority colleges (e.g., Gujarati Minority at Mithibai/UPG, Sindhi at KC/Jai Hind, South Indian at SIES). If you fall into any of these categories, your 70% could get you into a top-3 college easily.</li><li><strong>The \"In-House\" Advantage:</strong> If you did your 12th (Junior College) from a specific college (like Thakur or Ruia), you get first preference for the degree college admission, often at a lower percentage.</li></ul><h3><strong>Checklist for your Application</strong></h3><ol><li><strong>Top Reach:</strong> KC College, Jai Hind (Apply anyway for the 3rd list).</li><li><strong>Realistic Targets:</strong> Bhavan\'s, UPG, VSIT, SIES (Nerul).</li><li><strong>Safe Bets:</strong> Thakur College (Kandivali), Pillai (Panvel), Model College (Dombivli).</li></ol><p><br></p>', NULL, 'College Guide', 'Best B.Sc. IT Colleges in Mumbai with 70% Marks in 12th', 'Discover the top B.Sc. CS and IT colleges in Mumbai for 2025. Get detailed insights on placements, fees, and campus life. Perfect for students with 70% in 12th PCM. Find your ideal college and start your tech career today!', 'BSc IT colleges Mumbai, BSc Computer Science Mumbai 2026, Mumbai University admission 2026, Mithibai college BSc IT placement, St Xaviers BSc CS cutoff, BSc IT colleges for 70 percent, best placements BSc CS Mumbai, Mumbai University merit list 2026', 'Best B.Sc. college in Mumbai', NULL, NULL, 1, 16, '2026-05-09 10:55:10', '2026-05-15 04:13:01');
INSERT INTO `academic_guides` (`id`, `user_id`, `title`, `slug`, `content`, `file_path`, `category`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `meta_keywords`, `target_university`, `target_course`, `featured_image`, `is_published`, `views`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(3, 5, 'AKTU B.Tech 2nd Year CSE/IT Syllabus PDF 2025-26 | Revised Curriculum', 'aktu-btech-2nd-year-cseit-syllabus-pdf-2025-26-revised-curriculum-b2evy', '<p>Aapke blog ya website ke liye ek structured aur professional content niche diya gaya hai. Isme keywords ko natural tarike se use kiya gaya hai taaki ye Google par rank kare aur students ke liye helpful ho.</p><h2><strong>AKTU B.Tech 2nd Year (CSE/IT) Syllabus 2025-26: Everything You Need to Know</strong></h2><p>Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Technical University (AKTU) has updated its curriculum for the 2nd-year B.Tech students in Computer Science and Information Technology. The second year is the most crucial phase of engineering as it introduces the <strong>Core Building Blocks</strong> of software development and system design.</p><h3><strong>Detailed Semester-wise Subject List</strong></h3><h4><strong>Semester 3: The Foundation of Logic</strong></h4><p>In the 3rd semester, the focus shifts from general engineering to core computing concepts.</p><ul><li><strong>Data Structures (DS):</strong> Learn how to organize and store data efficiently using Arrays, Linked Lists, and Trees.</li><li><strong>Computer Organization &amp; Architecture (COA):</strong> Understand how the hardware executes instructions and manages memory.</li><li><strong>Discrete Structures:</strong> The mathematical logic behind computer science, including set theory and graph theory.</li><li><strong>Mathematics-IV:</strong> Advanced probability and statistical techniques used in data analysis.</li></ul><h4><strong>Semester 4: Advanced Systems &amp; Programming</strong></h4><p>The 4th semester prepares you for industry-level programming and system management.</p><ul><li><strong>Operating Systems (OS):</strong> The heart of any device. Study process scheduling, deadlocks, and memory management.</li><li><strong>Object-Oriented Programming (Java):</strong> Master the principles of OOPs like Encapsulation, Inheritance, and Polymorphism using Java.</li><li><strong>Theory of Automata (TAFL):</strong> Understand the mathematical models of computation (Finite Automata, Turing Machines).</li><li><strong>Microprocessor / Python:</strong> Practical exposure to hardware programming or modern high-level scripting.</li></ul><h3><strong>Evaluation &amp; Credit Scheme</strong></h3><p>As per the latest AKTU guidelines, each core subject typically carries <strong>3 to 4 credits</strong>. To pass, students must maintain a balance between internal sessional marks and the external End-Semester Examination (ESE).</p><ul><li><strong>Internal Marks:</strong> Based on CT-1, CT-2, and Teacher’s Assessment.</li><li><strong>External Marks:</strong> 70 or 100 marks theory paper.</li><li><strong>Practical Labs:</strong> Dedicated labs for DS, Java, and OS are mandatory to gain hands-on experience.</li></ul><h3><strong>How to Download the Official PDF?</strong></h3><p>You can download the unit-wise detailed syllabus directly from the link provided below. This PDF includes the latest marking scheme, textbook recommendations (Standard authors like Galvin for OS or Cormen for Algorithms), and the lab manual index.</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778324321/zbzk3j2fo2leckvyrrpc.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'AKTU B.Tech 2nd Year CSE/IT Syllabus PDF 2025-26 | Revised Curriculum', 'Download the latest AKTU B.Tech 2nd Year (3rd & 4th Sem) CSE/IT Syllabus PDF. Get the complete evaluation scheme, unit-wise topics for Data Structures, OS, and Java, plus recommended books for the 2025-26 session.', 'aktu,  2nd year betch syllabus, 2nd year os,dbms,uhv,coa Syllabus , aktu syllabus pdf', 'AKTU', NULL, NULL, 1, 23, '2026-05-09 10:58:41', '2026-05-14 21:57:50'),
(4, 3, 'JMI BA LLB 2026 Mock Paper PDF - Full Syllabus Practice Test', 'jmi-ba-llb-2026-mock-paper-pdf-full-syllabus-practice-test-HiNqJ', '<p>If you are dreaming of getting into <strong>Jamia Millia Islamia (JMI)</strong> for <strong>BA LLB</strong>, you probably know how tough the entrance exam can be. I have noticed that a lot of students get really confused about where to start or what the actual paper looks like. That is why I have put together this <strong>Full Syllabus Mock Paper</strong> to give your preparation a real boost.</p><p>Here is why you should definitely try solving this mock:</p><ul><li><strong>Covers Everything:</strong> I have made sure to include important questions from Legal Aptitude, GK, English, and Reasoning. </li><li><br></li><li><strong>Fix Your Timing:</strong> Solving this will help you understand how to manage your speed so you don\'t run out of time during the real exam. </li><li><br></li><li><strong>Build Your Confidence:</strong> Once you finish these 50 questions, you will feel much more ready to take on the actual Jamia entrance. </li><li><br></li></ul><p>This is not an \"official\" paper, but I have designed it very carefully to help you build a strong base. Just take a deep breath, solve it honestly, and learn from the mistakes you make. </p><p><br></p><p>All the best, everyone! Keep working hard, and you will see yourself at the Jamia campus soon.</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778343346/ofawqrtwpwaccy0bjpeh.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'JMI BA LLB 2026 Mock Paper PDF - Full Syllabus Practice Test', 'Download the free JMI BA LLB 2026 Full Mock Paper PDF with Answer Key. Practice 50 high-yield MCQs for Jamia Millia Islamia Law Entrance Exam. Start preparing now!', 'JMI BA LLB 2026, Jamia Entrance Exam Mock, BA LLB Practice Paper, Law Entrance Preparation, MyCollegeVerse Mock Test, Jamia Law Admission, LLB Practice Questions, JMI Entrance 2026', 'Jamia Milia Islamia - JMI', NULL, NULL, 1, 37, '2026-05-09 16:15:46', '2026-05-14 17:14:43'),
(5, 3, 'JMI BA LLB 2026 Notes PDF – Everything You Need to Clear the Entrance', 'jmi-ba-llb-2026-notes-pdf-everything-you-need-to-clear-the-entrance-L9OFr', '<p>If you are planning to sit for the <strong>Jamia Millia Islamia (JMI) BA LLB entrance</strong> this year, you already know that the syllabus can feel like a lot. Most of us get stuck trying to find the right notes that actually match the Jamia exam pattern instead of just random law topics. </p><p><br></p><p>To make things easier, I’ve put together these <strong>BA LLB Entrance Notes in PDF</strong>. These aren’t just copies from textbooks; they are made specifically for students who want to focus on what actually gets asked in the exam. </p><p><br></p><p><strong>Why these notes will help you:</strong></p><ul><li><strong>To the Point:</strong> No extra fluff. You get exactly what you need for Legal Aptitude and GK. </li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li><strong>Easy Language:</strong> I’ve avoided hard legal jargon so you can understand complex topics quickly. </li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li><strong>Quick Revision:</strong> Since these are PDFs, you can read them on your phone or laptop whenever you have 10 minutes free. </li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li><strong>Focus on Important Areas:</strong> We’ve highlighted the parts that Jamia loves to ask about, like the Constitution and current legal affairs. </li></ul><p><br></p><p>Don\'t let the competition scare you. If your basics are clear and you have the right material to study from, you’ve already won half the battle. Grab the PDFs, start your prep, and stay focused. </p><p><br></p><p>Good luck with your preparation! See you at the Jamia campus.</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778343595/ptzai9xlg7qx2b4o6fod.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'JMI BA LLB 2026 Notes PDF – Everything You Need to Clear the Entrance', 'Stop wasting time looking for study material everywhere. Get these simple, easy-to-read JMI BA LLB notes in PDF. We’ve covered Legal, GK, and Reasoning specifically for the Jamia pattern to help you score better.', 'Jamia LLB entrance notes, JMI BA LLB study material 2026, Jamia law entrance preparation, BA LLB notes pdf download, JMI law entrance guide, Jamia entrance important topics, mycollegeverse law notes', 'Jamia Milia Islamia - JMI', NULL, NULL, 1, 56, '2026-05-09 16:19:55', '2026-05-15 06:41:17'),
(6, 3, 'JMI BA LLB 2017-2025 Paper Analysis – Spot the Patterns and Score High', 'jmi-ba-llb-2017-2025-paper-analysis-spot-the-patterns-and-score-high-dQend', '<p>If you are getting ready for the <strong>Jamia BA LLB entrance</strong>, you probably know that looking at previous year papers is the best way to prepare. But just looking at them isn\'t enough. I’ve gone through the papers from <strong>2017 all the way to 2025</strong> to see how the exam has changed over the years. </p><p><br></p><p>Knowing these patterns can save you a lot of time because you’ll know exactly which areas to focus on. </p><p><br></p><p><strong>What we found in our 2017-2025 Analysis:</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Legal Reasoning Trends:</strong> From 2017 to 2025, the focus has slowly shifted from simple definitions to more logic-based Principle-Fact questions. </li><li><br></li><li><strong>Static GK vs. Current Affairs:</strong> Jamia still loves its history and constitution, but in the last 3-4 years, they have started asking more about recent landmark judgments. </li><li><br></li><li><strong>The Math Factor:</strong> While math is only 10 marks, the questions from 2020 onwards have become slightly more calculative. Don\'t skip your percentages and train speed logic! </li><li><br></li><li><strong>English Difficulty:</strong> The vocabulary level has been fairly consistent, but the Reading Comprehension passages are getting a bit longer each year. </li><li><br></li></ul><p><strong>Why you should check this analysis:</strong></p><p>Instead of just guessing what might come in the exam, you can see the actual <strong>weightage</strong> of different topics. It helps you understand if the exam is getting harder and what the \"safe score\" should be based on previous years\' cut-offs. </p><p><br></p><p>Solving these papers is good, but studying the <strong>analysis</strong> is what makes you smart. Grab the breakdown, see the trends for yourself, and start practicing the right way. </p><p><br></p><p>Best of luck! You’ve got this.</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778343822/wxeq6cxcwzxbfa3ztezv.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'JMI BA LLB 2017-2025 Paper Analysis – Spot the Patterns and Score High', 'Don\'t just solve old papers, understand them. We’ve analyzed JMI BA LLB entrance papers from 2017 to 2025 to show you which topics repeat and how the difficulty level is changing.', 'JMI BA LLB previous year papers, Jamia law entrance paper analysis, BA LLB 2017-2025 question papers, JMI entrance exam trends, Jamia law entrance solved papers, BA LLB last year paper analysis, mycollegeverse law prep', 'Jamia Milia Islamia - JMI', NULL, NULL, 1, 38, '2026-05-09 16:23:42', '2026-05-14 17:19:49'),
(8, 5, 'aktu test', 'aktu-test-soc7G', '<p>ff</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778437961/pr04rrrksqnptrelyodq.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'ssed', 'vssed', 'ssed', 'as', NULL, NULL, 1, 6, '2026-05-10 18:32:42', '2026-05-14 15:31:38'),
(9, 9, 'AKTU Python Programming Notes 2026 | Important Questions, PYQ Analysis & Exam Guide', 'aktu-python-programming-notes-2026-important-questions-pyq-analysis-exam-guide-i1i3T', '<p>Python Programming is one of the most important subjects in AKTU B.Tech 2nd year. Many questions in the exam are repeated from previous year papers. This guide helps students focus on the most important units, coding programs, file handling, NumPy, dictionaries, strings, and output-based questions.</p><p><br></p><p>The notes are written in simple language so students can revise quickly before exams. It also includes smart preparation tips, repeated PYQ topics, and the most expected questions for upcoming AKTU exams.</p><p><br></p><p>If you want to score better in Python Programming without wasting time on less important topics, this guide will help you prepare in a structured and easy way.</p><p><br></p><h1>MOST EXPECTED QUESTIONS FOR NEXT PAPER</h1><p>Very high probability:</p><ol><li>File handling program</li><li>String slicing</li><li>Dictionary student marks</li><li>NumPy reshape/matrix</li><li>Prime/fibonacci</li><li>Output prediction</li><li>Exception handling</li><li>Set/list common elements</li><li>Functions</li><li>Search/sorting&nbsp;</li></ol><p><br></p><p><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\"></p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778442580/imv8vyzpdut1eqmy1aoq.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'AKTU Python Programming Notes 2026 | Important Questions, PYQ Analysis & Exam Guide', 'Download AKTU Python Programming notes with important questions, repeated PYQs, unit-wise priority, coding programs, and smart exam preparation tips for B.Tech 2nd year students.', 'AKTU Python Programming Notes, CST005 Notes, AKTU Python PYQ, Python Programming Important Questions, AKTU BTech Python Guide, Python Programming Exam Notes, AKTU Python Syllabus, Python Coding Programs AKTU, AKTU 2nd Year Python Notes, Python Programming PYQ Analysis', 'AKTU', NULL, NULL, 1, 44, '2026-05-10 19:49:40', '2026-05-15 00:29:07');
INSERT INTO `academic_guides` (`id`, `user_id`, `title`, `slug`, `content`, `file_path`, `category`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `meta_keywords`, `target_university`, `target_course`, `featured_image`, `is_published`, `views`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(10, 9, 'JMI MSc Banking and Financial Analytics Entrance Exam Syllabus: Full Guide with Topics', 'jmi-msc-banking-and-financial-analytics-entrance-exam-syllabus-full-guide-with-topics-9nQWw', '<p>Hey, so you\'re prepping for the <strong>JMI MSc Banking and Financial Analytics entrance exam</strong>? This thing is super tough but doable if you hit the right <strong>syllabus topics</strong> and <strong>study materials</strong>. It\'s mostly MCQs on math, stats, reasoning, economics, banking basics, and some computer stuff. No official detailed list out there, but we pull from the program\'s own courses and past papers. Let\'s break it down simple, like I\'m just sharing notes from my study group.</p><p><br></p><h2>Quick Exam Pattern for JMI MSc BFA Entrance</h2><p>It\'s 80 questions in 2 hours, 80 marks total, minus 0.25 for wrong answers. 80% weight on test, 20% interview. You need grad degree with 55% and math/stats in 10+2 or college.</p><p><br></p><h2>Main Subjects in JMI MSc Banking Financial Analytics Syllabus</h2><p>Focus on these big areas – quant and stats take like 50-60% from what past papers show.</p><h2>1. Mathematics &amp; Statistics (Biggest Chunk!)</h2><p>This is from their MSBFA14 paper. Grind algebra, probability – they love matrices and distributions.</p><ul><li><strong>Linear Algebra</strong>: Vectors, matrix stuff like inverse/determinants, eigenvalues, quadratic forms.</li><li><strong>Probability</strong>: Random variables, binomial/Poisson/normal distributions, joint/conditional probs.</li><li><strong>Sampling</strong>: Z-test, t-test, chi-square, Central Limit Theorem, estimation tricks.</li></ul><p><strong>Study Tip</strong>: Grab NCERT 11-12 Math, or Simon &amp; Blume\'s book. Practice CAT-level problems.</p><p><br></p><h2>2. Financial Economics (MSBFA11)</h2><p>Markets and risk – think stock stuff, not too deep.</p><ul><li><strong>Markets</strong>: How they work, equity/funds/commodities, CAPM model, SEBI rules.</li><li><strong>Risk</strong>: Utility theory, risk aversion, stochastic dominance.</li><li><strong>Efficiency</strong>: EMH (efficient market hypothesis), financial inclusion via tech.</li></ul><p><strong>Books</strong>: Brealey Myers Corporate Finance basics, or Bodie Merton\'s Financial Economics.</p><p><br></p><h2>3. Banking Operations &amp; MIS (MSBFA12)</h2><p>Real banking lingo – payments, software.</p><ul><li><strong>Channels</strong>: ATM, net banking, mobile apps, RTGS/NEFT/MICR.</li><li><strong>MIS</strong>: CRM tools, bank KPIs, forex/treasury/risk management.</li><li><strong>Issues</strong>: E-banking risks, core banking software, Rangarajan report.</li></ul><p><strong>Go-To</strong>: Ramesh Singh Indian Economy for banking chapter.</p><p><br></p><h2>4. Computer &amp; Programming Basics (MSBFA13)</h2><p>Not coding test, just fundamentals.</p><ul><li><strong>Basics</strong>: Binary numbers, logic gates, computer architecture.</li><li><strong>Coding</strong>: Flowcharts, C language I/O/loops/functions/arrays/pointers/files.</li></ul><p><strong>Easy Prep</strong>: freeCodeCamp Python intro, or any Class 12 computer book.</p><p><br></p><h2>Reasoning &amp; Quant (Untouchable Part)</h2><p>Puzzles, series, data interpretation – RS Aggarwal book is gold. Arun Sharma for speed.</p><h2>Semester-Wise JMI MSc BFA Course Peek (Helps for Entrance Context)</h2><p>Program\'s 4 semesters, 82 credits. Entrance mirrors Semester 1 vibes.</p><ul><li><strong>Sem 1</strong>: Financial Eco, Banking Ops, Python/R programming, Math/Stats.</li><li><strong>Sem 2</strong>: Risk/Insurance, Corp Finance, Databases, Optimization.</li><li><strong>Sem 3</strong>: Portfolios, Econometrics, AI in Finance, electives like ML.</li><li><strong>Sem 4</strong>: Big industry project.</li></ul><h2>Best Study Materials for JMI BFA Entrance Syllabus</h2><ul><li><strong>Past Papers</strong>: Download M80 code papers (2020-2024) – see VaR, regression, matrices repeat.</li><li><strong>Books</strong>: RS Aggarwal Quant/Reasoning, Hogg Stats, Indian Economy by Ramesh.</li><li><strong>Online</strong>: YouTube solutions for 2024 paper, JMI prospectus PDF.</li><li><strong>Practice</strong>: 50-100 MCQs daily on <strong>quantitative aptitude for banking entrance</strong>, stats probs.</li></ul><p>Hit <a href=\"https://jmi.ac.in/ACADEMICS/Departments/Department-Of-Economics/Programmes/Courses/636\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: color(srgb 0.00392157 0.415686 0.443137); background-color: unset;\">JMI Economics Dept page</a> or their PDF for full deets. Most asked? Math/stats hands down. Study smart, you\'ll crack it! Good luck, bro.</p>', NULL, 'Syllabus', 'JMI MSc Banking and Financial Analytics Entrance Exam Syllabus: Full Guide with Topics,JMI MSc BFA entrance topics, JMI banking analytics entrance pattern, JMI BFA previous papers, JMI MSc finance entrance syllabus, quantitative aptitude JMI BFA', 'JMI MSc Banking & Financial Analytics entrance syllabus: detailed topics on math, stats, economics, banking ops. Past papers, books & prep tips for 2026 exam.', 'JMI MSc Banking Financial Analytics syllabus, JMI BFA entrance exam syllabus, MSc BFA JMI syllabus PDF,Jamia Millia Islamia MSc entrance, banking finance analytics syllabus, JMI economics MSc syllabus, stats math JMI entrance, JMI M80 paper analysis', 'JMI', NULL, NULL, 1, 21, '2026-05-10 20:15:50', '2026-05-14 15:33:37'),
(11, 3, 'JMI BA LLB Entrance Exam 2026: High-Quality Model Papers & Solved PYQs', 'jmi-ba-llb-entrance-exam-2026-high-quality-model-papers-solved-pyqs-FIk6H', '<h2><strong>How to Crack the JMI BA LLB Entrance Exam</strong></h2><p>If you are dreaming of studying law at Jamia Millia Islamia, you already know it is one of the top places to be. But getting in isn\'t easy. The competition is tough, and the questions can be tricky. The best way to feel ready is to practice with <strong>model question papers</strong> and <strong>previous year papers (PYQs)</strong>.</p><h3><strong>Why Practice with Model Papers?</strong></h3><p>Think of a model paper like a dress rehearsal before a big play. It helps you understand:</p><ul><li><strong>The Paper Pattern:</strong> You’ll see how many questions come from Legal Aptitude, English, and Current Affairs.</li><li><strong>Time Management:</strong> Can you finish 150 questions in time? Practice helps you get faster.</li><li><strong>Weak Spots:</strong> You might find out you\'re great at Math but need more work on Logical Reasoning.</li></ul><h3><strong>What’s inside a High-Quality Paper?</strong></h3><p>A good practice paper should feel just like the real exam. It needs to cover:</p><ol><li><strong>Legal Knowledge:</strong> Questions about the Constitution and basic laws.</li><li><strong>Current Affairs:</strong> Important news from the last year.</li><li><strong>General English:</strong> Grammar, vocabulary, and reading passages.</li><li><strong>Social Sciences:</strong> History and Geography questions that Jamia loves to ask.</li></ol><h3><strong>Tips for Your Preparation</strong></h3><p>Don\'t just read the answers—try to solve the paper without looking! Set a timer for 120 minutes and sit in a quiet room. Once you finish, check your mistakes and read the explanations. This is the fastest way to learn.</p><p>Keep practicing, stay calm, and you will do great. Jamia is waiting for you!</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778478860/ijqxvdctxiu5vs1kyp7u.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'JMI BA LLB Entrance Exam 2026: High-Quality Model Papers & Solved PYQs', 'Get the best JMI BA LLB model question papers and previous year papers. Boost your prep with high-quality practice tests designed for the Jamia law entrance exam.', 'Jamia BA LLB entrance model paper, JMI law entrance previous year papers, Jamia Millia Islamia law entrance prep, BA LLB solved papers JMI, best books for Jamia law entrance, JMI law entrance mock test 2026, Jamia law syllabus and pattern', 'Jamia Milia Islamia - JMI', NULL, NULL, 1, 30, '2026-05-11 05:54:21', '2026-05-15 05:49:14'),
(12, 5, 'JEE Main 2026 Rank 2.4 Lakh OBC-NCL Male: Best Colleges Through JoSAA, CSAB & Chhattisgarh Counselling', 'jee-main-2026-rank-24-lakh-obc-ncl-male-best-colleges-through-josaa-csab-chhattisgarh-counselling-YUzGj', '<p>With around <strong>2.4 lakh CRL rank</strong> in JEE Main and being <strong>OBC-NCL male from Chhattisgarh</strong>, you are realistically in the range where:</p><ul><li><strong>Top NITs / good IIIT CSE</strong> are not possible through normal JoSAA rounds.</li><li>But you still have chances in:</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">lower branches in some <strong>GFTIs</strong></li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">a few <strong>North-East NITs</strong></li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">some newer/lower IIITs in <strong>CSAB special rounds</strong></li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">state counselling in Chhattisgarh</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">decent private colleges through JEE score.</li></ul><p>Also important: in JoSAA, for OBC seats the cutoff is based on <strong>OBC category rank</strong>, not CRL.</p><p><br></p><h2>What you can realistically get</h2><p><br></p><h3><strong>1. Through JoSAA Counselling (realistic)</strong></h3><p><br></p><p><strong>At 2.4 lakh CRL, expect mainly:</strong></p><h4>Possible NIT options (mostly core/lower branches)</h4><ul><li>North-East NITs:</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">NIT Mizoram</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">NIT Nagaland</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">NIT Arunachal Pradesh</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">NIT Sikkim</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">NIT Meghalaya</li></ul><p>Branches you may get:</p><ul><li>Civil</li><li>Mechanical</li><li>Metallurgy</li><li>Production</li><li>Biotechnology</li><li>Chemical (some cases)</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong><u>Mostly in later JoSAA rounds or CSAB.</u></strong></p><h4>Possible IIITs (very limited, mostly CSAB)</h4><p>Some lower/new IIITs may close very high in CSAB special rounds:</p><ul><li>IIIT Bhagalpur</li><li>IIIT Manipur</li><li>IIIT Kalyani</li></ul><p>But likely only:</p><ul><li>ECE</li><li>Smart Manufacturing</li><li>lower-demand specializations</li></ul><p>Not guaranteed.</p><p><br></p><p>Reddit discussions from recent counselling cycles also show many students around similar ranges depending heavily on CSAB vacancy movement.</p><p><br></p><h4><strong>GFTIs </strong>— your best central counselling chance</h4><p><strong>You have better chances in:</strong></p><ul><li>Assam University</li><li>Gurukula Kangri Vishwavidyalaya</li><li>Birla Institute of Technology Mesra (less likely main campus, maybe extension campuses)</li><li>Sant Longowal Institute of Engineering and Technology</li></ul><p><strong>Branches:</strong></p><ul><li>Civil</li><li>Electrical</li><li>Mechanical</li><li>Food Tech</li><li>Production</li><li>Metallurgy</li></ul><p><br></p><h3><strong>2. Through CSAB Special Rounds — VERY IMPORTANT FOR YOU</strong></h3><p>You should definitely sit in <strong>CSAB</strong> after JoSAA.</p><p>Many seats remain vacant in:</p><ul><li>North-East NITs</li><li>GFTIs</li><li>lower-demand branches</li><li>newer IIITs</li></ul><p>Cutoffs move significantly in CSAB. Students with much higher ranks often get seats there compared to JoSAA regular rounds.</p><p>For your rank, CSAB is more important than JoSAA.</p><p><br></p><h3><strong>3. Chhattisgarh State Counselling (CGDTE) — strongest option</strong></h3><p>Since you are from Chhattisgarh, this is where you may get comparatively better colleges.</p><p><strong>Possible colleges:</strong></p><ul><li>Government Engineering College Raipur</li><li>Chhattisgarh Swami Vivekanand Technical University affiliated colleges</li><li>Shri Shankaracharya Technical Campus</li><li>Rungta College of Engineering and Technology</li></ul><p><strong>You may get:</strong></p><ul><li>CSE in lower private colleges</li><li>IT</li><li>AI/DS</li><li>ECE</li><li>Mechanical</li></ul><p>depending on counselling round.</p><p><br></p><h3>4. Private colleges accepting JEE Main</h3><p>At your rank you can still target:</p><ul><li>KIIT</li><li>SRM Institute of Science and Technology</li><li>Chandigarh University</li><li>UPES</li></ul><p>Some offer scholarships based on JEE.</p><p><br></p><h3><strong>What I would recommend practically</strong></h3><p><br></p><h3>Best strategy for you:</h3><ol><li>Fill <strong>JoSAA completely</strong></li><li>Definitely participate in <strong>CSAB</strong></li><li>Prioritize:</li></ol><ul><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">NIT tag over branch only if fees/location acceptable</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">otherwise take CSE/IT in decent state/private college</li></ul><ol><li>Do not leave counselling because many seats move late.</li></ol><p><br></p><h3><strong>Reality check</strong></h3><p><strong>At 2.4 lakh CRL:</strong></p><ul><li>Top NIT/IIIT CSE is not realistic.</li><li>But a <strong>government engineering seat is still possible</strong>.</li><li>If your priority is placements/software career, then:</li><li>decent local CSE college + coding skills</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">can be better than low-demand branch in remote NIT.</li></ul><p><br></p><p>Useful official cutoff sources:</p><ul><li><a href=\"https://josaa.admissions.nic.in/applicant/seatmatrix/openingclosingrankarchieve.aspx?utm_source=chatgpt.com\" target=\"_blank\">JoSAA Opening &amp; Closing Ranks</a></li><li><a href=\"https://josaa.admissions.nic.in/Applicant/seatallotmentresult/currentorcr.aspx?utm_source=chatgpt.com\" target=\"_blank\">Current JoSAA OR-CR Portal</a></li></ul><p><br></p>', NULL, 'Syllabus', 'JEE Main 2026 Rank 2.4 Lakh OBC-NCL Male: Best Colleges Through JoSAA, CSAB & Chhattisgarh Counselling', 'Find realistic college options for 2.4 lakh rank in JEE Main for OBC-NCL male candidates from Chhattisgarh. Explore JoSAA, CSAB, NIT, IIIT, GFTI, and Chhattisgarh state counselling opportunities with accurate cutoff-based insights.', '2.4 lakh rank college predictor jee mains, jee mains 240000 rank colleges, obc ncl 2.4 lakh rank colleges, colleges for 2.4 lakh rank in jee mains, josaa colleges for 240000 rank, csab colleges for 2.4 lakh rank, nit colleges for low jee mains rank, gfti colleges for 240000 rank, chhattisgarh engineering counselling colleges, jee mains obc ncl college predictor, which college can i get with 2.4 lakh rank in jee mains, best colleges for obc ncl male with 240000 rank, can i get nit with 2.4 lakh rank, csab special round colleges for low rank, government engineering colleges through jee mains 240000 rank, colleges available in josaa after 2 lakh rank, low cutoff nit colleges for obc category, colleges in chhattisgarh accepting jee mains score, cse colleges for 2.4 lakh jee rank, private colleges accepting 2.4 lakh jee mains rank, josaa counselling for 240000 rank, csab counselling cutoff for obc ncl, jee mains counselling colleges list, chhattisgarh state counselling engineering colleges, engineering colleges through csab special rounds, home state quota colleges chhattisgarh, nit seat chances with low rank, gfti admission through jee mains, nit lower branch vs private cse, should i take core branch in nit, best private engineering colleges with low jee rank, government vs private engineering college, is csab worth it for low rank, best branch for placements at low rank, jee mains rank vs college, engineering college predictor india, jee mains cutoff analysis, obc ncl engineering admission, engineering admission 2026, affordable engineering colleges india, top colleges for low jee mains percentile, jee counselling guide for low rank students', NULL, NULL, NULL, 1, 13, '2026-05-11 07:04:41', '2026-05-15 00:30:42'),
(13, 9, 'AKTU Engineering Chemistry Notes for B.Tech 1st Year | PYQ Based Smart Guide by MyCollegeVerse', 'aktu-engineering-chemistry-notes-for-btech-1st-year-pyq-based-smart-guide-by-mycollegeverse-SZ6fS', '<p>This Engineering Chemistry guide is for <strong>AKTU </strong>B.Tech 1st year students who want clear and short notes for exams. Yahan par water technology, electrochemistry and corrosion, fuels and lubricants, polymers and nanomaterials, spectroscopy and green chemistry sab simple points me diye gaye hain. Content exam pattern aur PYQ trend ko dhyan me rakh kar arrange kiya gaya hai, taki aap pehle high weightage topics revise kar sako.</p><p><br></p><p>Water technology section me hardness of water, lime soda process, zeolite process, ion exchange method, boiler problems aur basic environmental chemistry cover hai. Iske baad electrochemistry and batteries wale part me electrochemical cell, Nernst equation, EMF numericals, dry cell, lead acid battery aur corrosion prevention ke short notes milege. In dono units se AKTU engineering chemistry paper me har saal strong questions aate hain, isliye inhe first priority deni chahiye.</p><p><br></p><p>Fuels and lubricants unit me aapko classification of fuels, gross and net calorific value, bomb calorimeter numericals, lubricant properties jaise viscosity, flash and fire point, cloud and pour point clear language me milenge. Polymers and engineering materials unit me thermoplastic and thermosetting polymers, PVC, Bakelite, Nylon 6,6, cement composition and setting, alloys aur basic nanomaterials topics simple definition plus uses ke saath diye gaye hain. Ye sab topics direct theory questions ke liye useful hain aur scoring bhi hote hain.</p><p><br></p><p>Spectroscopy and atomic or molecular theory part me Beer Lambert law, UV and IR spectroscopy basics, molecular orbital diagrams of O2 and N2, bond order aur magnetic nature jaise important concepts explain kiye gaye hain. Green chemistry principles bhi short points me add kiye gaye hain, jo AKTU BTech first year chemistry me short notes ke roop me baar baar puchhe jaate hain. Is poore guide ka aim hai ki aapko engineering chemistry notes ek hi jagah mil jaye jo exam ke just pehle revise ho sake.</p><p><br></p><p>Agar aap AKTU engineering chemistry important questions, BTech 1st year chemistry PYQ ya unit wise notes search kar rahe ho, to ye page aapke liye ek compact smart guide ban sakta hai. Aap isko MyCollegeVerse ke dusre tools jaise notes section aur planner ke saath use karke apna revision plan bana sakte ho. Simple language, clear headings aur exam focused content ki wajah se ye guide first year students ke liye helpful rahega.</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778510854/c72siqhbov6ab46axmx7.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'AKTU Engineering Chemistry Notes for B.Tech 1st Year | PYQ Based Smart Guide by MyCollegeVerse', 'AKTU B.Tech 1st year Engineering Chemistry notes and PYQ based guide. Water, electrochemistry, fuels, polymers, nanomaterials, spectroscopy and green chemistry covered in simple language.', 'AKTU engineering chemistry notes, BTech 1st year chemistry AKTU, engineering chemistry important questions, AKTU chemistry PYQ, water technology notes AKTU, electrochemistry and corrosion AKTU, fuels and lubricants AKTU, polymers and nanomaterials AKTU, spectroscopy Beer Lambert law AKTU, green chemistry principles AKTU, MyCollegeVerse chemistry guide, AKTU BTech first year syllabus chemistry', 'AKTU', NULL, NULL, 1, 14, '2026-05-11 14:47:35', '2026-05-14 15:32:53'),
(16, 9, 'Master Java & OOP: AKTU B.Tech Exam Guide | MyCollegeVerse', 'master-java-oop-aktu-btech-exam-guide-mycollegeverse-5SfVL', '<p>Look, the AKTU syllabus is basically a boss fight you didn\'t prepare for. Thick books, zero time, and that one random topic from Unit 5 that always shows up for 10 marks. At <strong>MyCollegeVerse.in</strong>, we get it. We’re not here to give you a lecture—we’re here to help you clear the \"back\" and actually understand what’s going on.</p><p><br></p><h3>1. JDK vs JRE vs JVM (The Big Three)</h3><p>Forget the textbook definitions for a second. If Java was a <strong>Zomato order</strong>:</p><ul><li><strong>JVM:</strong> This is the guy actually cooking. He takes the instructions (bytecode) and turns them into something you can eat (output).</li><li><strong>JRE:</strong> This is the whole kitchen. It’s got the cook (JVM) and all the spices/utensils (libraries) needed to finish the dish.</li><li><strong>JDK:</strong> This is the entire restaurant chain. It’s got the kitchen, the cook, and the tools to invent <em>new</em> recipes (the compiler).</li></ul><blockquote><strong>Exam Tip:</strong> If the question asks for a diagram, just draw the nested boxes: JDK contains JRE, and JRE contains JVM. Easy marks.</blockquote><h3><br></h3><h3>2. The Four OOP Pillars (The 10-Mark Must-Knows)</h3><p>If you don\'t know these, don\'t even enter the exam hall.</p><ul><li><strong>Encapsulation:</strong> Like a capsule pill. You don\'t need to see the medicine inside; the plastic shell keeps it safe. It’s all about data hiding.</li><li><strong>Inheritance:</strong> Basically, \"Baap ka, Bete ka.\" The child class gets everything from the parent class so you don\'t have to rewrite code like a maggu.</li><li><strong>Polymorphism:</strong> Think of the word \"Cut.\" A barber cuts hair, a director cuts a scene, and a cricketer cuts the ball. Same word, totally different vibes.</li><li><strong>Abstraction:</strong> Your car\'s steering wheel. You turn it, the car moves. You don\'t care about the internal combustion or gear ratios. Just the essentials.</li></ul><h3><br></h3><h3>3. Exception Handling (The \"Don\'t Crash\" Plan)</h3><p>Programs are like our weekend plans—they break.</p><ul><li><strong>Try:</strong> \"I\'m gonna try to run this sketchy code.\"</li><li><strong>Catch:</strong> \"Oops, it failed. Instead of a \'Blue Screen of Death,\' show this error message.\"</li><li><strong>Finally:</strong> \"I don\'t care if it worked or failed, just close the file/turn off the light.\"</li></ul><h3><br></h3><h3>4. Multithreading &amp; Collections</h3><p><strong>Multithreading</strong> is just your brain during an exam—one \"thread\" is writing the answer, another is checking the time, and a third is wondering what’s for lunch. It’s about doing stuff in parallel so the CPU doesn\'t sit idle.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Collections</strong> are just fancy storage bins.</p><ul><li>Need speed? <strong>ArrayList.</strong></li><li>Need a chain? <strong>LinkedList.</strong></li><li>Hate duplicates? <strong>HashSet.</strong></li></ul><p><br></p><h3> The \"One Night Before\" Strategy</h3><p>AKTU loves its diagrams. Even if your code is a bit shaky, draw a neat flow-chart or a class diagram. Start with <strong>Multithreading</strong> and <strong>Exception Handling</strong>—these are almost guaranteed 10-markers.</p><p><br></p><p>Don\'t just mug up; try to explain it to your roommate. If they get it, the examiner will too.</p><p><strong>Cheers, and go crush those externals!</strong> <em>Building Academic Identity — The Student OS</em></p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778649037/x64fpdzwy72pneeexewn.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'Master Java & OOP: AKTU B.Tech Exam Guide | MyCollegeVerse', 'Ace your AKTU B.Tech exams with these concise, exam-oriented notes on Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) with Java. Covers JVM, Inheritance, Exception Handling.', 'Java important topics AKTU, Explain Polymorphism with diagram, Encapsulation, Master Java & OOP', 'AKTU', NULL, NULL, 1, 219, '2026-05-13 05:10:38', '2026-05-15 05:47:15'),
(17, 9, 'AKTU PPS Notes: B.Tech 1st Year Important PYQs', 'aktu-pps-notes-btech-1st-year-important-pyqs-37K6i', '<p>PPS ka syllabus bada lag raha hai? Tension mat lo. Humne AKTU ke pichle 5 saal ke papers nichod kar yeh <strong>Smart Prep Doc</strong> banaya hai. Isme wahi programs hain jo exam mein har saal aate hain. Pura syllabus chatne ki zaroorat nahi hai, bas in high-scoring topics ko pakdo aur exam phodo!</p><p><br></p><p>Is guide mein humne sirf wahi topics aur programs rakhe hain jo baar-baar exam mein aate hain—jaise Prime numbers, Bubble Sort, aur Pointers. Yeh koi boring textbook nahi hai, balki aapka exam nikalne ka shortcut hai. Agar aapne abhi tak kuch nahi padha, toh bas in selected programs ko practice kar lo, 40+ marks kahin nahi gaye!</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Unit 1 (Basics):</strong> \"Isse sirf short questions aate hain. Algorithm aur Flowchart ke beech ka difference yaad kar lo, 5-10 minute ka kaam hai.\"</p><p><strong>Unit 2 (Loops):</strong> \"Yeh sabse zyada scoring area hai. Prime, Palindrome aur Fibonacci—in teeno mein se ek pakka ayega. Logic ratne ki jagah flow samjho.\"</p><p><strong>Unit 4 (Arrays &amp; Sorting):</strong> \"Matrix multiplication thoda lamba lag sakta hai, par step-by-step likhoge toh full marks milenge. Bubble sort ka code har saal repeat hota hai, ise skip mat karna.\"</p>', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778685924/g1oyxo8sw7vv6e3qhqp8.pdf', 'Syllabus', 'AKTU PPS Notes: B.Tech 1st Year Important PYQs', 'Score 40+ in AKTU PPS. Unit-wise important programs, C logic, and last 5 years solved questions for B.Tech 1st year students.', 'AKTU PPS Notes B.Tech 1st Year,Programming for Problem Solving C Notes, AKTU PPS Important Questions, PPS PYQ Solutions, C Programming AKTU 1st Year.', 'AKTU', NULL, NULL, 1, 37, '2026-05-13 15:25:25', '2026-05-15 06:16:15');
INSERT INTO `academic_guides` (`id`, `user_id`, `title`, `slug`, `content`, `file_path`, `category`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `meta_keywords`, `target_university`, `target_course`, `featured_image`, `is_published`, `views`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(23, 9, 'AKTU Digital Communication Notes: KEC601 Important PYQs', 'aktu-digital-communication-notes-kec601-important-pyqs-TVVw5', '<!DOCTYPE html>\r\n<html lang=\"en\">\r\n<head>\r\n  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />\r\n  <title>Digital Communication (KEC601) – AKTU Smart Prep | MyCollegeVerse</title>\r\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1\" />\r\n\r\n  <meta name=\"description\" content=\"Digital Communication (KEC601/BEC601) smart prep notes for AKTU. Covers digital modulation, line coding, information theory, error control coding, probability and noise based on last 4–5 years papers.\" />\r\n\r\n  <link href=\"https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins:wght@500;700&family=Lato:wght@300;400;700&display=swap\" rel=\"stylesheet\" />\r\n\r\n  <style>\r\n    :root {\r\n      --primary: #1d3557;\r\n      --accent: #00b4d8;\r\n      --accent-soft: #edf2ff;\r\n      --bg: #f7f9fc;\r\n      --text: #1f2933;\r\n      --muted: #6b7b8d;\r\n      --card-bg: #ffffff;\r\n      --radius-lg: 18px;\r\n      --radius-sm: 10px;\r\n      --shadow-soft: 0 10px 30px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.12);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    * {\r\n      box-sizing: border-box;\r\n      margin: 0;\r\n      padding: 0;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    body {\r\n      font-family: \"Lato\", system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, \"Segoe UI\", sans-serif;\r\n      background: radial-gradient(circle at top left, #e0f4ff 0, #f7f9fc 40%, #ffffff 100%);\r\n      color: var(--text);\r\n      line-height: 1.6;\r\n      padding: 24px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    a {\r\n      color: var(--accent);\r\n      text-decoration: none;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .doc {\r\n      max-width: 960px;\r\n      margin: 0 auto 40px auto;\r\n      background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.98);\r\n      border-radius: 24px;\r\n      box-shadow: var(--shadow-soft);\r\n      overflow: hidden;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /* Header / Branding */\r\n    .doc-header {\r\n      background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--primary), #14213d);\r\n      color: #ffffff;\r\n      padding: 24px 32px;\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      align-items: center;\r\n      justify-content: space-between;\r\n      gap: 16px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-left {\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      flex-direction: column;\r\n      gap: 4px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 22px;\r\n      font-weight: 700;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.03em;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-tagline {\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.12em;\r\n      color: #e0fbfc;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-pill {\r\n      padding: 6px 12px;\r\n      border-radius: 999px;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25);\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.12em;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /* Hero */\r\n    .hero {\r\n      padding: 32px;\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 3fr) minmax(0, 2fr);\r\n      gap: 24px;\r\n      align-items: center;\r\n      border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      background: linear-gradient(135deg, #ffffff, #e6f4ff);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 30px;\r\n      font-weight: 700;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-subtitle {\r\n      font-size: 15px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      margin-bottom: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-tags {\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      flex-wrap: wrap;\r\n      gap: 8px;\r\n      margin-bottom: 16px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .tag {\r\n      background: var(--accent-soft);\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n      padding: 6px 10px;\r\n      border-radius: 999px;\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.08em;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-meta {\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card {\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-lg);\r\n      padding: 18px 20px;\r\n      box-shadow: 0 12px 28px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.12);\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.35);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 16px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      margin-bottom: 10px;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card-list {\r\n      list-style: none;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card-list li {\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /* Body */\r\n    .doc-body {\r\n      padding: 24px 32px 32px 32px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .section-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 20px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      margin-bottom: 8px;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .section-subtitle {\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      margin-bottom: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .contents-grid {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(190px, 1fr));\r\n      gap: 12px;\r\n      margin-bottom: 32px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-chip {\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-sm);\r\n      padding: 10px 12px;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      flex-direction: column;\r\n      gap: 3px;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-chip-title {\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-chip-meta {\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-section {\r\n      margin-bottom: 32px;\r\n      padding: 18px 18px 20px 18px;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-lg);\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.35);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-header {\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      align-items: center;\r\n      justify-content: space-between;\r\n      gap: 12px;\r\n      margin-bottom: 12px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-heading {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 17px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-weight {\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.08em;\r\n      border-radius: 999px;\r\n      padding: 5px 10px;\r\n      background: var(--accent-soft);\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-body {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 3fr) minmax(0, 2fr);\r\n      gap: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-block-title {\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      font-weight: 700;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.08em;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-list {\r\n      padding-left: 16px;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-list li {\r\n      margin-bottom: 4px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .code-patterns {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: 1fr;\r\n      gap: 10px;\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .code-card {\r\n      background: #f8fafc;\r\n      border-radius: 12px;\r\n      padding: 10px 12px;\r\n      border: 1px dashed rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.6);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .code-card-title {\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      margin-bottom: 4px;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .two-column {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.1fr) minmax(0, 1fr);\r\n      gap: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .card-soft {\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-lg);\r\n      padding: 14px 16px;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .step-list,\r\n    .resource-list {\r\n      padding-left: 16px;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .step-list li,\r\n    .resource-list li {\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .doc-footer {\r\n      padding: 16px 32px 22px 32px;\r\n      border-top: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      justify-content: space-between;\r\n      flex-wrap: wrap;\r\n      gap: 8px;\r\n      background: #f9fbff;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    @media (max-width: 768px) {\r\n      body {\r\n        padding: 12px;\r\n      }\r\n      .hero,\r\n      .unit-body,\r\n      .two-column {\r\n        grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr);\r\n      }\r\n      .doc-header {\r\n        flex-direction: column;\r\n        align-items: flex-start;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  </style>\r\n</head>\r\n<body>\r\n\r\n<div class=\"doc\">\r\n\r\n  <!-- Header -->\r\n  <header class=\"doc-header\">\r\n    <div class=\"brand-left\">\r\n      <div class=\"brand-title\">MyCollegeVerse.in</div>\r\n      <div class=\"brand-tagline\">Building Academic Identity · The Student OS</div>\r\n    </div>\r\n    <div class=\"brand-pill\">AKTU · ECE/EE · Digital Communication (KEC601)</div>\r\n  </header>\r\n\r\n  <!-- Hero -->\r\n  <section class=\"hero\">\r\n    <div>\r\n      <h1 class=\"hero-title\">Digital Communication – Smart Prep Doc</h1>\r\n      <p class=\"hero-subtitle\">\r\n        AKTU B.Tech VI semester Digital Communication (KEC601/BEC601) notes based on last 4–5 years question papers and unit-wise PYQs.\r\n      </p>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"hero-tags\">\r\n        <span class=\"tag\">Digital Modulation</span>\r\n        <span class=\"tag\">Information Theory</span>\r\n        <span class=\"tag\">Error Control Coding</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <p class=\"hero-meta\">\r\n        Focus pehle digital modulation aur information theory par rakho, phir line coding, error control coding aur probability ke numericals. \r\n        Ye document mainly exam pattern aur frequently asked questions ke hisaab se design kiya gaya hai.\r\n      </p>\r\n    </div>\r\n\r\n    <aside class=\"hero-card\">\r\n      <div class=\"hero-card-title\">What This Doc Covers</div>\r\n      <ul class=\"hero-card-list\">\r\n        <li>• Unit-wise list of most asked topics in Digital Communication.</li>\r\n        <li>• Short notes style points for theory questions.</li>\r\n        <li>• Hints for common numericals (entropy, PDF/CDF, Hamming code).</li>\r\n      </ul>\r\n    </aside>\r\n  </section>\r\n\r\n  <!-- Body -->\r\n  <main class=\"doc-body\">\r\n\r\n    <!-- Contents -->\r\n    <section>\r\n      <h2 class=\"section-title\">Contents</h2>\r\n      <p class=\"section-subtitle\">\r\n        Units are sorted by exam importance, not only by syllabus order.\r\n      </p>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"contents-grid\">\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit A – Probability & Random Variables</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">PDF, CDF, mean, variance, random process basics.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit B – Baseband & Line Coding ★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">Line codes, ISI, Nyquist, eye diagram.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit C – Information Theory & Source Coding ★★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">Entropy, mutual information, Huffman coding, Shannon limit.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit D – Digital Modulation ★★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">ASK/FSK/PSK, BPSK, QPSK, DPSK, error probability.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit E – Error Control Coding ★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">Block codes, Hamming codes, code rate, CRC.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit F – Noise, Channels & Misc</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">Noise types, SNR, AWGN channel, DC system block diagram.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit D – Digital Modulation -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"modulation\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit D – Digital Modulation (Very High Priority)</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★★ · Core of paper</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Most Asked Concepts</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Basic idea of digital modulation: mapping bits to changes in amplitude, frequency or phase.</li>\r\n            <li>ASK, FSK and PSK: principle, waveforms, block diagrams, advantages and disadvantages.</li>\r\n            <li>BPSK and QPSK: constellation diagrams, bit mapping, bandwidth and bit rate relationship.</li>\r\n            <li>Differential PSK (DPSK): concept and basic waveform explanation.</li>\r\n            <li>Comparison of digital modulation schemes in terms of bandwidth efficiency and power efficiency.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Typical Exam Questions</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Explain ASK, FSK and PSK with neat waveforms. Write their advantages and disadvantages.</li>\r\n            <li>Explain BPSK and QPSK with constellation diagrams and bit mapping.</li>\r\n            <li>Compare different digital modulation schemes in terms of bandwidth and noise performance.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Diagram Hints – Constellation</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>BPSK: 2 points on real axis, one at +A and one at −A.</li>\r\n              <li>QPSK: 4 points at (±A, ±A) or at four quadrants with 90° phase shifts.</li>\r\n              <li>Axes label as In-phase (I) and Quadrature (Q) for clarity.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Answer Writing Tips</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Har scheme ke liye: definition → expression → waveform sketch → advantages/disadvantages pattern follow karo.</li>\r\n              <li>Comparison wale answers me chota table bana do (rows: bandwidth, SNR, complexity, use).</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit C – Information Theory -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"info-theory\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit C – Information Theory & Source Coding</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★★ · Very frequently asked</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Key Theory Points</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Self-information of a symbol and basic idea of information content.</li>\r\n            <li>Entropy of a discrete memoryless source, properties of entropy and entropy of a binary source.</li>\r\n            <li>Mutual information, channel capacity and Shannon’s channel coding theorem.</li>\r\n            <li>Source coding: coding efficiency, redundancy and need of source coding.</li>\r\n            <li>Huffman coding algorithm: steps to construct code and compute average length.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Typical Questions & Numericals</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Define entropy and derive expression for entropy of a binary source. State its properties.</li>\r\n            <li>Explain mutual information and channel capacity. State Shannon–Hartley law.</li>\r\n            <li>Construct Huffman code for a given set of symbols and probabilities. Calculate average code length and efficiency.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Huffman Coding Pattern</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Step 1: Arrange symbols in increasing order of probability.</li>\r\n              <li>Step 2: Merge lowest probability pair and repeat until one node remains.</li>\r\n              <li>Step 3: Assign 0/1 to branches and read codewords from root to leaf.</li>\r\n              <li>Step 4: Compute average code length and efficiency.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Entropy & Capacity Tips</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Remember entropy formula H = −∑ p log₂ p.</li>\r\n              <li>For binary source max entropy is 1 bit when p = 0.5.</li>\r\n              <li>Channel capacity expressions (for binary symmetric channel, etc.) key formulas ko ek sheet pe likh ke revise karo.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit B – Baseband & Line Coding -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"line-coding\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit B – Baseband Transmission & Line Coding</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Core Topics</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Block diagram of a digital communication system and explanation of each block.</li>\r\n            <li>Line coding: need of line codes and requirements of a good line code (zero DC, timing content, bandwidth).</li>\r\n            <li>Types of line codes: unipolar NRZ, polar NRZ, RZ, Manchester, differential Manchester, bipolar AMI.</li>\r\n            <li>Pulse shaping, intersymbol interference (ISI) and Nyquist criterion for zero ISI.</li>\r\n            <li>Eye diagram: construction, meaning of eye opening and effect of noise and ISI.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Common Questions</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Explain different types of line coding schemes with waveforms.</li>\r\n            <li>What is ISI? State and explain Nyquist criterion for zero ISI.</li>\r\n            <li>What is an eye diagram? How does it help in judging the performance of a digital communication system?</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Diagram Hints – Eye Diagram</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Draw overlapping pulses over two symbol intervals and show “eye” opening region.</li>\r\n              <li>Label vertical opening (noise margin) and horizontal opening (timing jitter margin).</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Answer Tips</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Block diagram answers me har block (source encoder, channel encoder, modulator, channel, demodulator, etc.) ko ek short line me explain karo.</li>\r\n              <li>Line codes ke waveforms simple square pulses se bana sakte ho; zyada artistic drawing ki zaroorat nahi.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit A – Probability & RV -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"probability\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit A – Probability & Random Variables</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★ (mostly numericals + short)</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Key Concepts</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Basic probability definitions: sample space, event, conditional probability.</li>\r\n            <li>Probability density function (PDF) and cumulative distribution function (CDF); relationship and properties.</li>\r\n            <li>Mean (expectation), second moment, variance and standard deviation for random variables.</li>\r\n            <li>Short notes on random processes, stationarity and ergodicity (if present in syllabus version).</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Numerical Patterns</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Given a PDF, find mean and variance by integration.</li>\r\n            <li>Given a CDF, derive the corresponding PDF.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Exam Tips</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Formula list bana ke rakh: mean, variance, PDF/CDF relations.</li>\r\n              <li>Numericals me steps clearly dikhayo – definition likho, phir integration steps.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit E – Error Control Coding -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"coding\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit E – Error Control Coding</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Important Topics</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Block codes: code word, code length, message length, code rate.</li>\r\n            <li>Minimum distance of a code and its relation to error detection and correction capability.</li>\r\n            <li>Hamming codes: construction of (7,4) code, parity bits positions, syndrome calculation.</li>\r\n            <li>Parity check codes, checksum and cyclic redundancy check (CRC) concepts.</li>\r\n            <li>Basic idea of convolution coding and simple encoder diagram.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Typical Questions</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Explain Hamming code and construct a (7,4) Hamming code for given data.</li>\r\n            <li>Define code rate and minimum distance. How many errors can be detected and corrected by a code?</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Hamming Code Hints</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Remember positions of parity bits (1,2,4,...) and data bits.</li>\r\n              <li>Use parity check equations to compute parity bits and to find single-bit error position.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit F – Noise & Channels + Strategy -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"noise\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit F – Noise, Channels & Overall Strategy</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★ · Supportive</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Short Notes Topics</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Types of noise: thermal noise, shot noise, white noise, etc.</li>\r\n            <li>Signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) definition and its effect on digital communication.</li>\r\n            <li>AWGN channel basics and role of noise in eye diagram closure.</li>\r\n            <li>Block diagram of a digital communication system.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Score Strategy (Fast)</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Step 1: Master digital modulation and information theory (core long questions).</li>\r\n              <li>Step 2: Do line coding, eye diagram and error control coding (Hamming).</li>\r\n              <li>Step 3: Cover probability numericals and small noise/channel notes for short questions.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Overall Strategy -->\r\n    <section>\r\n      <h2 class=\"section-title\">How to Use This Doc for Exams</h2>\r\n      <p class=\"section-subtitle\">\r\n        Use this sheet as a map: first clear the heaviest blocks, then fill small theory gaps.\r\n      </p>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"two-column\">\r\n        <div class=\"card-soft\">\r\n          <h3 class=\"unit-block-title\">Revision Flow</h3>\r\n          <ol class=\"step-list\">\r\n            <li>Day 1–2: Digital modulation (ASK/FSK/PSK, BPSK, QPSK) + core definitions and diagrams.</li>\r\n            <li>Day 3: Information theory + Huffman coding numericals.</li>\r\n            <li>Day 4: Line coding, ISI, Nyquist, eye diagram.</li>\r\n            <li>Day 5: Error control coding (Hamming, block code basics).</li>\r\n            <li>Last 1–2 days: Probability numericals + short notes on noise & channels and complete PYQ practice.</li>\r\n          </ol>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"card-soft\">\r\n          <h3 class=\"unit-block-title\">MyCollegeVerse Resources</h3>\r\n          <ul class=\"resource-list\">\r\n            <li><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in/notes\" target=\"_blank\">MyCollegeVerse Notes</a> – explore more subject-wise notes and prep docs.</li>\r\n            <li><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in\" target=\"_blank\">MyCollegeVerse.in</a> – tools, notes and academic utilities for AKTU students.</li>\r\n            <li><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in/resume/build\" target=\"_blank\">Resume Builder</a> – once semester ends, build your resume quickly.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n  </main>\r\n\r\n  <footer class=\"doc-footer\">\r\n    <span>© MyCollegeVerse.in – The Student OS</span>\r\n    <span>Designed as a smart prep doc for AKTU Digital Communication (KEC601/BEC601)</span>\r\n  </footer>\r\n\r\n</div>\r\n\r\n</body>\r\n</html>', NULL, 'Notes', 'AKTU Digital Communication Notes: KEC601 Important PYQs', 'Master AKTU Digital Communication (KEC601). Unit-wise notes on Modulation, Information Theory, and Huffman Coding with last 5 years solved patterns', 'aktu notes', 'AKTU', NULL, NULL, 1, 20, '2026-05-13 17:02:03', '2026-05-15 01:33:25');

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `ai_usages`
--

CREATE TABLE `ai_usages` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `model` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `type` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `topic` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `prompt_tokens` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `candidates_tokens` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `total_tokens` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `metadata` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`metadata`)),
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
