--
-- Table structure for table `assignments`
--

CREATE TABLE `assignments` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `recruiter_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `job_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `description` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `instructions` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `role` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `task_type` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'General',
  `submission_types` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`submission_types`)),
  `deadline` datetime DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_public` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  `status` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
  `settings` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`settings`)),
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `assignments`
--

INSERT INTO `assignments` (`id`, `recruiter_id`, `job_id`, `title`, `slug`, `description`, `instructions`, `role`, `task_type`, `submission_types`, `deadline`, `is_public`, `status`, `settings`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(3, 1, NULL, 'Content writer intern', 'content-writer-intern', '<h1><br></h1><h2>Objective</h2><p>To evaluate your ability to create engaging, well-structured, and SEO-friendly educational content.</p><p><br></p><h2> Task 1: Blog Writing</h2><h3>Topic</h3><p><strong>“Importance of Public Speaking Skills for Students”</strong></p><p><br></p><h3>Word Count</h3><p>600–800 words</p><p><br></p><h3>Requirements</h3><ul><li>Include a clear <strong>introduction, body, and conclusion</strong></li><li>Use proper <strong>headings (H1, H2, H3)</strong> for structure</li><li>Maintain <strong>simple, student-friendly language</strong></li><li>Ensure the content is <strong>100% original (no plagiarism)</strong></li><li>Add <strong>real-life examples or practical scenarios</strong></li><li>Keep the tone <strong>informative with a slightly conversational touch</strong></li></ul><p><br></p><h2> Task 2: Basic SEO Optimization</h2><p>Please include the following SEO elements:</p><ul><li><strong>Primary Keyword:</strong></li><li> Public Speaking Skills for Students</li><li><strong>Secondary Keywords:</strong></li><li> (e.g., communication skills, confidence building, student development)</li><li><strong>Meta Title:</strong></li><li> Maximum 60 characters</li><li><strong>Meta Description:</strong></li><li> Maximum 150–160 characters</li><li><strong>Slug/URL:</strong></li><li> Example: <code>/public-speaking-skills-for-students</code></li></ul><h2><br></h2><h2> Submission Guidelines</h2><ul><li>Submit your assignment in <strong>Google Docs or Word format</strong></li><li>Ensure proper formatting (headings, spacing, readability)</li><li>Include the <strong>SEO section clearly at the end of the document</strong></li><li>Submission must be <strong>original (no AI-generated or copied content)</strong></li></ul><h2> Deadline</h2><p>		08/05/26</p><p><br></p><h2> Evaluation Criteria</h2><ul><li><strong>Content Quality &amp; Clarity</strong></li><li><strong>Structure &amp; Readability</strong></li><li><strong>Understanding of SEO Basics</strong></li><li><strong>Creativity &amp; Originality</strong></li></ul><p><br></p>', '<h1><br></h1><h2>Objective</h2><p>To evaluate your ability to create engaging, well-structured, and SEO-friendly educational content.</p><p><br></p><h2> Task 1: Blog Writing</h2><h3>Topic</h3><p><strong>“Importance of Public Speaking Skills for Students”</strong></p><p><br></p><h3>Word Count</h3><p>600–800 words</p><p><br></p><h3>Requirements</h3><ul><li>Include a clear <strong>introduction, body, and conclusion</strong></li><li>Use proper <strong>headings (H1, H2, H3)</strong> for structure</li><li>Maintain <strong>simple, student-friendly language</strong></li><li>Ensure the content is <strong>100% original (no plagiarism)</strong></li><li>Add <strong>real-life examples or practical scenarios</strong></li><li>Keep the tone <strong>informative with a slightly conversational touch</strong></li></ul><p><br></p><h2> Task 2: Basic SEO Optimization</h2><p>Please include the following SEO elements:</p><ul><li><strong>Primary Keyword:</strong></li><li> Public Speaking Skills for Students</li><li><strong>Secondary Keywords:</strong></li><li> (e.g., communication skills, confidence building, student development)</li><li><strong>Meta Title:</strong></li><li> Maximum 60 characters</li><li><strong>Meta Description:</strong></li><li> Maximum 150–160 characters</li><li><strong>Slug/URL:</strong></li><li> Example: <code>/public-speaking-skills-for-students</code></li></ul><h2><br></h2><h2> Submission Guidelines</h2><ul><li>Submit your assignment in <strong>Google Docs or Word format</strong></li><li>Ensure proper formatting (headings, spacing, readability)</li><li>Include the <strong>SEO section clearly at the end of the document</strong></li><li>Submission must be <strong>original (no AI-generated or copied content)</strong></li></ul><h2> Deadline</h2><p>		08/05/26</p><p><br></p><h2> Evaluation Criteria</h2><ul><li><strong>Content Quality &amp; Clarity</strong></li><li><strong>Structure &amp; Readability</strong></li><li><strong>Understanding of SEO Basics</strong></li><li><strong>Creativity &amp; Originality</strong></li></ul><p><br></p>', 'content writer intern', 'General', '[\"link\",\"file\",\"text\"]', '2026-05-08 21:34:00', 1, 'active', NULL, '2026-05-06 16:04:33', '2026-05-06 16:04:33'),
(4, 1, NULL, 'Content Writer Intern', 'content-writer-intern-1', '<h2><strong>Objective</strong></h2><p>To evaluate your ability to create engaging, well-structured, and SEO-friendly educational content.</p><p><br></p><h2><strong>Task 1: Blog Writing</strong></h2><h3><strong>Topic</strong></h3><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">“Importance of Public Speaking Skills for Students”</strong></p><h3><strong>Word Count</strong></h3><p>600–800 words</p><h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3><ul><li>Include a clear <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">introduction, body, and conclusion</strong></li><li>Use proper <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">headings (H1, H2, H3)</strong> for structure</li><li>Maintain <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">simple, student-friendly language</strong></li><li>Ensure the content is <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">100% original (no plagiarism)</strong></li><li>Add <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">real-life examples or practical scenarios</strong></li><li>Keep the tone <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">informative with a slightly conversational touch</strong></li></ul><p><br></p><h2><strong>Task 2: Basic SEO Optimization</strong></h2><p>Please include the following SEO elements:</p><ul><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Primary Keyword:</strong></li><li>Public Speaking Skills for Students</li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Secondary Keywords:</strong></li><li>(e.g., communication skills, confidence building, student development)</li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Meta Title:</strong></li><li>Maximum 60 characters</li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Meta Description:</strong></li><li>Maximum 150–160 characters</li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Slug/URL:</strong></li><li>Example: <code class=\"ql-font-monospace\" style=\"background-color: rgb(241, 245, 249);\">/public-speaking-skills-for-students</code></li></ul><h2><br></h2><h2><strong>Submission Guidelines</strong></h2><ul><li>Submit your assignment in <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Google Docs or Word format</strong></li><li>Ensure proper formatting (headings, spacing, readability)</li><li>Include the <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">SEO section clearly at the end of the document</strong></li><li>Submission must be <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">original (no AI-generated or copied content)</strong></li></ul><h2><strong>Deadline</strong></h2><p>08/05/26</p><h2><strong>Evaluation Criteria</strong></h2><ul><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Content Quality &amp; Clarity</strong></li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Structure &amp; Readability</strong></li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Understanding of SEO Basics</strong></li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Creativity &amp; Originality</strong></li></ul><p><br></p>', '<h2><strong>Objective</strong></h2><p>To evaluate your ability to create engaging, well-structured, and SEO-friendly educational content.</p><p><br></p><h2><strong>Task 1: Blog Writing</strong></h2><h3><strong>Topic</strong></h3><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">“Importance of Public Speaking Skills for Students”</strong></p><h3><strong>Word Count</strong></h3><p>600–800 words</p><h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3><ul><li>Include a clear <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">introduction, body, and conclusion</strong></li><li>Use proper <strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">headings (H1, H2, H3)</strong> for structure</li><li>Maintain 42);\">informative with a slightly conversational touch</strong></li></ul><p><br></p><h2><strong>Task 2: Basic SEO Optimization</strong></h2><p>Please include the following SEO elements:</p><ul><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Primary Keyword:</strong></li><li>Public Speaking Skills for Students</li><li><strong style=\"color: rgb(15, 23, 42);\">Secondary -- Table structure for table `assignment_evaluations`

CREATE TABLE `assignment_evaluations` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `submission_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `criteria` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `score` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `feedback` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Table structure for table `assignment_submissions`
--

CREATE TABLE `assignment_submissions` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `assignment_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `candidate_name` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `candidate_email` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `candidate_phone` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `submission_link` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `file_path` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `file_id` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `submission_text` longtext DEFAULT NULL,
  `recruiter_notes` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `score` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `status` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  `expires_at` datetime DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `assignment_submissions`
--

(10, 3, NULL, 'Olusami Mary', 'olusamimary2005@gmail.com', '+2349029718151', 'https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Z4nQ0Eip4i2gtdlpuYYHEvgI38ZHE6Q0R7r29RaKIG0/edit?usp=drivesdk', NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, 'pending', '2026-06-07 15:47:45', '2026-05-08 15:47:45', '2026-05-08 15:47:45'),
(11, 3, NULL, 'Nida Noorain', 'freelancernoorain@gmail.com', '7330466235', 'https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_IZdUYaYDhKxlg08zCx9qGaebmaNt4dP/edit?usp=drivesdk&ouid=117716982188829438155&rtpof=true&sd=true', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778257677/vgrxulw1mduamq9jlfxl.pdf', 'vgrxulw1mduamq9jlfxl', NULL, 'she have written by own - human written score fit well but have to work on strucutre and seo implementation , need editorial guidance with well written structure and grammer', 75, 'reviewed', '2026-05-18 16:27:58', '2026-05-08 16:27:58', '2026-05-09 06:01:40'),

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `blogs`
--

CREATE TABLE `blogs` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `category_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `content` text NOT NULL,
  `excerpt` varchar(500) DEFAULT NULL,
  `featured_image` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `featured_image_alt` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `meta_title` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `meta_description` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `meta_keywords` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `seo_score` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `ai_score` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `auto_recommend_colleges` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  `college_ids` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`college_ids`)),
  `is_published` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `views` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `published_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `blogs`
--

INSERT INTO `blogs` (`id`, `user_id`, `category_id`, `title`, `slug`, `content`, `excerpt`, `featured_image`, `featured_image_alt`, `meta_title`, `meta_description`, `meta_keywords`, `seo_score`, `ai_score`, `auto_recommend_colleges`, `college_ids`, `is_published`, `views`, `published_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(1, 3, NULL, 'MyCollegeVerse: The Ultimate Platform to Find Roommates & Friends Before College Starts', 'collegeverse-the-ultimate-platform-to-find-roommates-friends-before-college-starts', '<p>Starting college is exciting—but let’s be honest, it can also feel overwhelming. From finding the right roommate to making new friends, students often struggle before even stepping onto campus. That’s where platforms like <strong>CollegeVerse</strong> step in to simplify your transition.</p><p>If you\'re searching for <i>“how to find college roommates online”</i> or <i>“best apps to make friends before college”</i>, this guide will show you why CollegeVerse is becoming a go-to solution for students.</p><h2>What is CollegeVerse?</h2><p><strong>CollegeVerse</strong> is a student-focused networking platform designed to help incoming college students connect with their future classmates even before the academic year begins. It primarily uses <strong>Instagram-based class communities</strong> where students can introduce themselves, share interests, and interact easily.</p><p>Unlike traditional platforms, it focuses specifically on <strong>pre-college social connections</strong>, helping students feel comfortable and prepared.</p><h2>Why Students Need Platforms Like CollegeVerse</h2><p>Before college starts, most students search for:</p><ul><li>“How to find roommates before college”</li><li>“Best ways to make friends in college”</li><li>“College networking platforms for freshmen”</li></ul><p>CollegeVerse directly solves these problems by creating a <strong>digital community for your specific college and class year</strong>.</p><h2>Key Features of CollegeVerse</h2><h3>1. Find Your Perfect Roommate</h3><p>CollegeVerse allows students to browse profiles, bios, and interests to match with compatible roommates before move-in day. This reduces stress and awkward first interactions.</p><h3>2. Build Friendships Early</h3><p>Instead of starting college alone, students can:</p><ul><li>Connect with classmates</li><li>Join conversations</li><li>Find people with similar interests</li></ul><p>This helps build a <strong>support system even before classes begin</strong>.</p><h3>3. Instagram-Based College Communities</h3><p>The platform uses dedicated Instagram pages for each college, making it:</p><ul><li>Easy to use</li><li>Familiar for Gen Z users</li><li>Highly interactive</li></ul><p>Students can post, engage, and discover others in their batch effortlessly.</p><h3>4. Stay Updated with Campus Life</h3><p>Students can also learn about:</p><ul><li>Campus groups</li><li>Events</li><li>Student activities</li></ul><p>This ensures you don’t miss out on opportunities right from day one.</p><h3>5. Unlimited Posting with Subscription</h3><p>CollegeVerse offers a subscription model where users can:</p><ul><li>Post multiple times</li><li>Stay visible</li><li>Keep profiles updated</li></ul><p>This increases chances of finding better connections.</p><h2>Benefits of Using CollegeVerse</h2><h3>✔ Reduces First-Day Anxiety</h3><p>Knowing people beforehand makes campus life smoother.</p><h3>✔ Faster Social Integration</h3><p>You already have friends when classes begin.</p><h3>✔ Better Roommate Matching</h3><p>Avoid random roommate assignments and find someone compatible.</p><h3>✔ Stronger Campus Network</h3><p>Build connections that last throughout your college journey.</p>', 'Keywords Targeted in This Article\r\nfind college roommates online\r\nmake friends before college\r\ncollege networking platform\r\nstudent community apps\r\ncollege roommate finder', 'https://ik.imagekit.io/studycubsfranchise/chat/chat_4_1777226325_Yhks5EIlfv?tr=w-600,q-80', 'MyCollegeVerse: The Ultimate Platform to Find Roommates & Friends Before College Starts', 'MyCollegeVerse: The Ultimate Platform to Find Roommates & Friends Before College Starts', 'CollegeVerse: The Ultimate Platform to Find Roommates & Friends Before College Starts', 'education', 30, 0, 0, '[]', 1, 73, '2026-04-14 15:07:50', '2026-04-14 15:07:08', '2026-05-15 06:23:13'),
(5, 3, 3, 'NEET UG 2026 Cancelled: NTA Official Re-Exam Date & CBI Probe News', 'breaking-neet-ug-2026-officially-cancelled-nta-confirms-re-exam-after-huge-paper-leak-scandal', '<p>So, it’s official. May 12, 2026, is officially the worst day of the year. The NTA finally pulled the plug. The NEET UG exam we all suffered through on May 3? <strong>Cancelled. Scrapped. Gone.</strong></p><p>Over 22 lakhs of us are basically back to square one because some \"solver gangs\" decided to turn our futures into a business. If you feel like screaming, you’re in good company.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3><strong>What actually happened? (The real tea)</strong></h3><p>Basically, the \"guess papers\" on Telegram weren\'t fake. The Rajasthan SOG found a handwritten paper in Jaipur that had <strong>120 questions</strong>—mostly Bio and Chemistry—matching the real exam perfectly. Some guy named <strong>Manish Yadav</strong> is already in jail for this, but the damage is done.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>The CBI has officially taken over now because this leak wasn\'t just \"local\"—it was a full-blown operation involving coaching hubs in Sikar and Jaipur. NTA said they \"couldn\'t allow the exam to stand\" because the fairness was totally trashed. No kidding.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3><strong>The \"Re-NEET\" Nightmare</strong></h3><p>Look, we all knew this was coming once the CBI got involved. Here’s what you need to know without the boring government fluff:</p><p><strong>When is it?</strong> No date yet, but everyone is pointing at <strong>June 2026</strong>. So yeah, goodbye to whatever summer plans you had.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Do we pay again?</strong> No. They better not even ask. NTA says the re-exam is free and they might even refund the first fee (I\'ll believe it when I see it).</p><p><strong>New Admit Cards:</strong> Your old one is trash. They’ll issue new ones soon. Keep an eye on neet.nta.nic.in.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>My Advice? Just Rant.</strong></h2><p>I\'m not gonna give you some \"keep your head up\" speech. This situation is absolute garbage.</p><p><strong>Close the books for 48 hours:</strong> Seriously. Your brain is fried. If you try to study Bio right now, you\'ll just get angry at the paper.</p><p><strong>Delete the \"Leak\" Groups:</strong> All those Telegram channels promising \"real papers\" for the re-exam? Block them. They’re the reason we’re in this mess.</p><p><strong>Wait for the NTA Notification:</strong> Don\'t trust every random YouTuber with a \"BREAKING NEWS\" thumbnail. Most of them are just farming views off our stress.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3><strong>Quick Answers (FAQ)</strong></h3><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Is it cancelled for everyone?</strong>&nbsp;</p><p>Yes. The whole thing. Nationwide.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Will the next paper be harder?</strong>&nbsp;</p><p>Probably not harder, but the security is going to be like a war zone. Expect 4 layers of checking.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>What about my May 3 score?</strong>&nbsp;</p><p>It doesn\'t exist anymore. NTA isn\'t even checking those OMRs.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3><strong>Final Word</strong></h3><p>It’s a nightmare, but at least those kids who bought the paper for 30 lakhs just lost their money. That’s the only win we have today. Stay safe, vent in the comments, and let’s just get through this.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Are you guys starting the grind again tomorrow, or are you taking a break? Let\'s talk.</strong></p><p>#NEET2026 #ExamCancelled #JusticeForStudents #ReNEET #NTAUpdate #CBIProbe #mycollegeverse</p>', 'NEET 2026 Exam Cancelled Official News: On May 12, the National Testing Agency (NTA) confirmed that the May 3rd examination stands scrapped. Following the CBI investigation into the NEET 2026 paper leak, evidence from Rajasthan\'s SOG proved that over 120 questions were compromised. The mastermind, Manish Yadav, has been detained in Jaipur, sparking nationwide calls for a fair Re-NEET.', '/blogs/breaking-neet-ug-2026-officially-cancelled-nta-confirms-re-exam-after-huge-paper-leak-scandal-1778587476_1Fs-dnqic', 'BREAKING: NEET UG 2026 Officially Cancelled! NTA Confirms Re-Exam After Huge Paper Leak Scandal', 'NEET UG 2026 Cancelled: NTA Official Re-Exam Date & CBI Probe News', 'NEET 2026 is officially cancelled! NTA confirms a Re-NEET in June following a CBI investigation into the Jaipur paper leak. Get new dates & refund details here.', 'NEET 2026 cancelled, NEET paper leak news, Re-NEET June 2026 date, CBI investigation NEET 2026, NTA official notice 12 May, NEET UG re-exam updates', 50, 100, 0, '[]', 1, 60, '2026-05-12 12:06:22', '2026-05-12 12:04:39', '2026-05-14 16:25:32'),
(6, 3, 4, 'AKTU Notes PDF Hub 2026: Semester-wise B.Tech Notes & PYQs', 'aktu-notes-pdf-hub-2026-semester-wise-btech-notes-pyqs', '<p><strong>Table of Contents:</strong></p><ol><li>Introduction: The AKTU Reality Check</li><li>AKTU 1st Year Notes (Physics, Chemistry, Maths, BEE, PPS)</li><li>Branch-specific Core Notes (CSE, IT, ECE, ME)</li><li>Quantum Series vs. Handwritten Notes: Which one to choose?</li><li>How to Download Unit-wise AKTU Notes PDF?</li><li>Strategy to score 8.5+ SGPA using these notes.</li><li>Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) by AKTU Students.</li></ol><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>1. Introduction: The AKTU Reality Check</strong></h2><p>AKTU (Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Technical University) ke exams ke baare mein ek baat famous hai:&nbsp;<i>\"Yahan padhna mushkil nahi hai, lekin scoring karna ek technique hai.\"</i></p><p>Har saal lakho students internet par&nbsp;<strong>AKTU Notes PDF</strong> search karte hain, lekin unhe milti hain purani photocopy ki scans. MyCollegeVerse ka maqsad hai aapko wo \"Premium\" notes dena jo toppers ne khud banaye hain. Ye notes strictly&nbsp;<strong>Latest 2026 Syllabus</strong> par based hain, taaki aapka time waste na ho.</p><h2><strong>2. AKTU 1st Year Notes: Building the Foundation</strong></h2><p>First year sabhi branches ke liye common hota hai, aur yahi wo saal hai jahan sabse zyada \"Back\" aati hai (especially Maths 2 aur Physics mein).</p><h3><strong>Engineering Mathematics (I &amp; II)</strong></h3><p>Maths mein sirf formulas ratne se kaam nahi chalega. Humne hamare notes mein&nbsp;<strong>Step-by-Step solutions</strong> diye hain.</p><ul><li><strong>Important Topics:</strong> Matrices, Eigenvalues, aur Multivariable Calculus.</li><li><strong>Search Intent Tip:</strong> Agar aap&nbsp;aktu maths 1 important questions dhoond rahe hain, toh hamare PDF ke end mein \"Top 20 Repeated Questions\" ki list check karein.</li></ul><h3><strong>Engineering Physics &amp; Chemistry</strong></h3><p>Physics mein Quantum Mechanics aur Wave Optics students ko kaafi pareshan karti hai. Hamare notes mein simplified&nbsp;<strong>Derivations</strong> hain jo AKTU exams mein har saal 10-marks mein aati hain.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>3. Branch-wise Hub: CSE, IT, ECE, &amp; ME</strong></h2><p>Jab aap 2nd year mein enter karte hain, tab asli engineering shuru hoti hai. Yahan \"General Study\" kaam nahi aati, yahan \"Subject Depth\" chahiye.</p><h3><strong>Computer Science &amp; IT (The Coding Core)</strong></h3><ul><li><strong>Data Structures (DSA):</strong> AKTU mein DSA ka paper conceptual zyada hota hai. Humne notes mein&nbsp;<strong>Dry Run</strong> ke saath algorithms samjhaye hain.</li><li><strong>Operating Systems (OS):</strong>&nbsp;aktu os notes pdf search karne walo ke liye humne Unit-wise bifurcation kiya hai.&nbsp;<strong>Process Scheduling</strong> aur&nbsp;<strong>Paging</strong> ko diagrams ke saath samjhaya gaya hai.</li><li><strong>Database Management Systems (DBMS):</strong> SQL queries aur Normalization ke bina paper adhura hai.</li></ul><h3><strong>Electronics &amp; Communication (ECE)</strong></h3><ul><li><strong>Digital System Design (DSD):</strong> K-Maps aur Logic Gates ke shortcuts jo aapka time bachayenge.</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>4. AKTU Quantum PDF vs. Handwritten Notes: The Ultimate Comparison</strong></h2><p>Ye har student ka dought hota hai. Let’s break it down:</p><ul><li><strong>Quantum Series:</strong> Ye un students ke liye best hai jo exam se sirf 2 din pehle kitab uthate hain. Isme questions ka collection accha hota hai lekin explanation kaafi \"bookish\" aur boring hoti hai.</li><li><strong>MyCollegeVerse Handwritten Notes:</strong> Humne topper\'s psychology ko use kiya hai. Humne wahi likha hai jo ek examiner answer sheet mein dekhna chahta hai. Hamare notes mein&nbsp;<strong>Keywords Underlined</strong> hain taaki aapko revision mein 5 minute se zyada na lage.</li><li>&nbsp;</li></ul><h2><strong>5. 8.5+ SGPA Strategy: Sirf Notes kaafi nahi hain!</strong></h2><p>Agar aapne&nbsp;aktu notes pdf download kar liye hain, toh agla step hai unhe use kaise karein?</p><ol><li><strong>The 5-Year Rule:</strong> Notes padhne ke baad pichle 5 saal ke&nbsp;<strong>AKTU PYQs</strong> uthayein. Aap dekhenge ki 70% questions repeat ho rahe hain.</li><li><strong>Diagrammatic Representation:</strong> AKTU mein examiner copy \"padhta\" nahi hai, wo copy \"dekhta\" hai. Jitne zyada clean diagrams aur flowcharts honge, utne zyada marks milenge.</li><li><strong>Unit Selection:</strong> Pehle Unit 1, 2 aur 5 khatam karein. Ye units scoring hoti hain aur inka weightage zyada hota hai.</li></ol><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>6. How to Download Unit-wise AKTU Notes?</strong></h2><p>Download karna bahut simple hai:</p><ol><li>Login karien Dashoard par &nbsp;diye gaye&nbsp;<a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in/notes\"><strong>Notes Tab /Section </strong></a>button par click karein.</li><li>Apna&nbsp;<strong>Semester</strong> select karein.</li><li>Subject ke saamne diye gaye&nbsp;<strong>\"Download PDF\"</strong> link par click karein. (Saare notes Google Drive par hosted hain, toh loading fast hogi).</li><li>&nbsp;</li></ol><h2><strong>7. IS AKTU Notes ARE &nbsp;FREE?</strong></h2><p>Yes All Notes of mycollegeverse are free for students!</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>8. Unit-Wise Deep Dive: Kya Padhein aur Kya Chhodein?</strong></h2><p>Sirf PDF dena kafi nahi hai, humne har main subject ka \"Analysis\" kiya hai taaki aapka effort sahi direction mein ho.</p><h3><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in/notes?search=os&amp;course_id=1&amp;semester=All&amp;is_verified=&amp;exam_trusted=\"><strong>Operating Systems (OS)</strong></a><strong> - [Subject Code: BCC401]</strong></h3><p>Operating System ka paper AKTU mein hamesha \"Conceptual\" aata hai.</p><ul><li><strong>Unit 1 (Introduction):</strong> Yahan se&nbsp;<i>System Calls</i> aur&nbsp;<i>Kernel</i> par 10 marks ka question fix hai.</li><li><strong>Unit 2 (Process Management):</strong>&nbsp;<i>CPU Scheduling Algorithms</i> (SJF, Round Robin) ke numericals zaroor practice karein. Hamare notes mein inka \"Dry Run\" step-by-step diya gaya hai.</li><li><strong>Unit 3 (Deadlocks):</strong>&nbsp;<i>Banker’s Algorithm</i>—iske bina paper nahi banta.</li><li><strong>Unit 4 (Memory Management):</strong>&nbsp;<i>Paging vs Segmentation</i> aur&nbsp;<i>Page Replacement Algorithms</i> (LRU, FIFO) par focus karein.</li></ul><h3><strong>Data Structures (DSA) - [Subject Code: BCS301]</strong></h3><p>DSA mein sabse badi galti students ye karte hain ki wo sirf Algorithm ratte hain, par C-Code nahi likhte.</p><ul><li><strong>Unit 1:</strong>&nbsp;<i>Time Complexity</i> aur&nbsp;<i>Array Operations</i>.</li><li><strong>Unit 2 (Stacks &amp; Queues):</strong>&nbsp;<i>Infix to Postfix conversion</i> ka logic hamare notes mein short-trick ke saath hai.</li><li><strong>Unit 3 (Linked Lists):</strong>&nbsp;<i>Doubly Linked List</i> ke insertion/deletion ka logic diagram ke saath samjhein.</li><li><strong>Unit 4 &amp; 5 (Trees &amp; Graphs):</strong>&nbsp;<i>B-Trees, AVL Trees,</i> aur&nbsp;<i>Dijkstra’s Algorithm</i>—ye toppers ke favorite topics hain.</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>9. AKTU 2nd Year (Semester 3 &amp; 4) - The Turning Point</strong></h2><p>2nd year mein hi students ko lagta hai ki engineering tough hai. Iska solution hai&nbsp;<strong>\"Syllabus Mapping.\"</strong> Humne har subject ke liye dedicated columns banaye hain:</p><figure class=\"table\"><table><tbody><tr><td><strong>Subject Name</strong></td><td><strong>Importance Level</strong></td><td><strong>Best Resource</strong></td><td><strong>Study Time Required</strong></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Discrete Maths</strong></td><td>Very High</td><td>MyCollegeVerse Notes</td><td>10 Days</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Computer Org (COA)</strong></td><td>Medium</td><td>NPTEL + Our PDF</td><td>7 Days</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Python Programming</strong></td><td>High (Lab + Theory)</td><td>Practice-based</td><td>5 Days</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Technical Communication</strong></td><td>Low (Scoring)</td><td>Last 2 Days Prep</td><td>2 Days</td></tr></tbody></table></figure><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>10. AKTU Exam Day Checklist: Last 24 Hours Strategy</strong></h2><p>Agar aapne pura saal kuch nahi padha aur ab sirf ek raat bachi hai, toh ye follow karein:</p><ol><li><strong>Skip Unit 4 &amp; 5 (If bulky):</strong> Pehle Unit 1, 2, aur 3 ko perfect karein. AKTU ka pattern aisa hai ki aap in 3 units se bhi 60-70% marks la sakte hain.</li><li><strong>Quantum ke \"Short Questions\":</strong> Har Unit ke peeche diye gaye 2-marks wale questions ko ghot kar pee jayein. Ye seedhe 20 marks confirm karte hain.</li><li><strong>Flowchart Practice:</strong> Theory ko paragraphs mein yaad karne ki jagah uska \"Block Diagram\" yaad karein.</li></ol><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>11. Resource Comparison: Why MyCollegeVerse is Winning?</strong></h2><p>Market mein bahut saare platforms hain like&nbsp;<i>Sanjivani, Quantum, ya Tech-Neo</i>. Par MyCollegeVerse kyun?</p><ul><li><strong>No Obsolete Content:</strong> Hum har semester ke baad \"Outdated\" topics ko remove kar dete hain.</li><li><strong>Mobile-Friendly PDFs:</strong> Hamare PDFs compressed hain, taaki low network mein bhi exam hall ke bahar download ho sakein.</li><li><strong>Direct Access:</strong> No annoying ads or pop-ups. Seedhe Google Drive access.</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>12. Final Thoughts for AKTU Batch 2026</strong></h2><p>Engineering sirf degree lene ka naam nahi hai, par AKTU jaise system mein survive karne ke liye aapko&nbsp;<strong>Smart Work</strong> ki zaroorat hai. Hamara&nbsp;<strong>AKTU Notes PDF Hub</strong> sirf ek resource nahi, balki aapka \"Exam Companion\" hai.</p><p><strong>Next Action:</strong> Agar aapko kisi specific subject (like Machine Learning ya Cyber Security) ke notes nahi mil rahe, toh niche comment karein ya hamare Telegram group ko join karein. Hum 24 ghante mein use upload kar denge.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>13. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)</strong></h2><p><strong>Q1. Kya ye notes 2026 syllabus ke liye updated hain?</strong> Yes, humne recently syllabus change ke baad saare links ko refresh kiya hai.</p><p><strong>Q2. Kya hume Quantum kharidne ki zaroorat hai?</strong> Agar aap hamare notes aur PYQs dhang se padh lete hain, toh aapko kisi aur book ya series ki zaroorat nahi padegi.</p><p><strong>Q3. Best YouTube channel for AKTU preparation?</strong> Notes ke saath-saath humne har subject ke niche best YouTube creators ki playlist bhi suggest ki hai.</p><p>&nbsp;</p>', 'Finding reliable study material for AKTU exams is a challenge. This hub provides 100% syllabus-mapped AKTU Notes PDF for 2026, including handwritten toppers\' notes, previous year papers, and last-minute revision guides for all B.Tech branches.', '/blogs/aktu-notes-pdf-hub-2026-semester-wise-btech-notes-pyqs-1778787684_n6YHjkso9', 'AKTU Notes PDF Hub 2026 Semester-wise B.Tech Notes & PYQs', 'AKTU Notes PDF Hub 2026: Semester-wise B.Tech Notes & PYQs', 'Download latest AKTU Notes PDF 2026 for B.Tech (CSE, IT, ECE, ME). Get unit-wise handwritten notes, Quantum guides, and PYQs to score 8+ SGPA. 100% Updated!', 'aktu notes pdf, aktu handwritten notes, aktu pyq pdf,  aktu important questions 2026,  aktu quantum pdf, btech notes for aktu', 70, 100, 0, '[]', 1, 53, '2026-05-14 19:42:22', '2026-05-14 19:41:27', '2026-05-15 06:21:38'),
(7, 3, NULL, 'How to find best college for you in 2026', 'how-to-find-best-college-for-you-in-2026', '<p>Hello everyone ever thought when it\'s come to finding best college for ourself according to our academic course / streams or branch then it\'s become too difficult to get landed into that fit.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>lets see how to find best college for you in 2026 or foe ourself as well.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>well sometimes we even get our desired college but then there tons of fees , extra fee, donation etc hostel fees and other , uff a lot of amount huh but what if we do not have that much of budget.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>How to find best college with great ROI?</strong></h2><p>&nbsp;</p><p>well it very important to always make balance in your dream college and there high amount fees and collection machine&nbsp;</p>', 'how to find best college for you under your suitable budget and near your hometown.', NULL, 'How to find best college for you in 2026', 'How to find best college for you in 2026', 'here are the list of best college for you based on your stream, interest and budget. get to know now', 'best college for you, budget college', 60, 100, 0, '[]', 0, 0, NULL, '2026-05-14 19:54:10', '2026-05-14 19:54:10'),
(8, 3, 3, 'How to Pass AKTU Exams in 7 Days: The Survival Guide', 'how-to-pass-aktu-exams-in-7-days-the-survival-guide', '<p>AKTU (Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Technical University) ke baare mein ek kadwa sach ye hai: Yahan wo nahi jeetta jo saal bhar padhta hai, balki wo jeetta hai jise&nbsp;<strong>\"Copy Bharne ki Art\"</strong> aur&nbsp;<strong>\"Sahi Content Picking\"</strong> aati hai.</p><p>Agar aapke paas sirf 7 din bache hain aur syllabus pahad jaisa dikh raha hai, toh panic mat kijiye. Is guide mein hum MyCollegeVerse ki special&nbsp;<strong>7-Day Sprint Strategy</strong> share kar rahe hain, jise follow karke average students ne bhi 8+ SGPA score kiya hai.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>1. The Psychology of an AKTU Examiner</strong></h2><p>Isse pehle ki aap padhna shuru karein, ye samjhein ki aapki copy check kaun kar raha hai. Ek examiner ke paas din mein saikdon (hundreds) copies aati hain. Unke paas har word padhne ka time nahi hota.</p><ul><li><strong>Visual Appeal:</strong> Wo dekhte hain diagrams, headings, aur clean writing.</li><li><strong>Keywords:</strong> Wo pure paragraph mein sirf \"Key Terms\" dhoondte hain.</li><li><strong>Quantity + Quality:</strong> AKTU mein sirf 2 page likhne se marks nahi milte, aapko 25-30 pages touch karne hote hain (sahi content ke saath).</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>2. The 7-Day Reverse Engineering Schedule</strong></h2><p>Hum 5 Units ko 7 din mein aise divide karenge ki revision ka bhi time mile.</p><h3><strong>Day 1 &amp; 2: The Scoring Pillars (Unit 1 &amp; Unit 2)</strong></h3><p>Zyadatar subjects mein Unit 1 aur 2 basics hote hain aur inka weightage 30-40 marks tak chala jata hai.</p><ul><li><strong>Focus:</strong> Definitions, Basic Derivations, aur Block Diagrams.</li><li><strong>Task:</strong> In do dinon mein in units ke saare&nbsp;<strong>Previous Year Questions (PYQs)</strong> ghot kar pee jayein.</li></ul><h3><strong>Day 3: The \"Deep Dive\" (Unit 4 ya 5)</strong></h3><p>Unit 3 aksar complex hoti hai, isliye hum use skip karke pehle Unit 4 ya 5 uthayenge (jo bhi choti aur scoring ho).</p><ul><li><strong>Strategy:</strong> In units se aksar 10-marks wale long questions fix hote hain. Unhe identify karein.</li></ul><h3><strong>Day 4: Numerical &amp; Problem Solving (If applicable)</strong></h3><p>Agar subject Maths, DBMS, ya COA hai, toh ye din sirf practice ke liye hai.</p><ul><li><strong>Pro-Tip:</strong> AKTU mein numerical agar poora sahi na bhi ho, toh&nbsp;<strong>Step Marking</strong> ke marks milte hain. Formula aur Given Data zaroor likhein.</li></ul><h3><strong>Day 5: The \"Pass Guarantee\" Unit (Unit 3)</strong></h3><p>Ab bachi hui unit uthayein. Isme sirf wahi padhein jo pichle 5 saal mein kam se kam 3 baar pucha gaya ho.</p><h3><strong>Day 6: The \"Quantum\" Scan &amp; Short Questions</strong></h3><p>AKTU ke paper mein&nbsp;<strong>Section A (2-marks questions)</strong> game changer hota hai.</p><ul><li><strong>Task:</strong> Quantum ya hamare notes se saare Units ke short questions ek baar dekh lein. Ye 20 marks confirm karte hain.</li></ul><h3><strong>Day 7: Full Length Mock &amp; Presentation Practice</strong></h3><p>Padhna band karein aur ye dekhein ki aap 3 ghante mein content deliver kaise karenge.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>3. The \"Answer Sheet\" Framework: Marks Kaise Layein?</strong></h2><p>Ye hamari website ka&nbsp;<strong>Main USP</strong> hai—asli strategy jo koi nahi batata.</p><h3><strong>A. Diagram is King</strong></h3><p>Agar question 5 marks ka hai, toh 1 diagram. Agar 10 marks ka hai, toh kam se kam 2 diagrams. Diagram ko hamesha pencil se banayein aur bada banayein (half-page size).</p><h3><strong>B. The \"Heading-Subheading\" Method</strong></h3><p>Kabhi bhi lambe paragraphs mat likhiye. Content ko points mein divide karein.</p><ul><li><i>Introduction</i></li><li><i>Working Principle</i></li><li><i>Block Diagram</i></li><li><i>Advantages &amp; Disadvantages</i></li><li><i>Applications</i></li></ul><h3><strong>C. Keyword Highlighting</strong></h3><p>Jab aap answer likh lein, toh black pen ya pencil se&nbsp;<strong>Important Keywords</strong> ko underline kar dein. Examiner ki nazar seedhe wahan jayegi aur wo full marks de dega.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>4. Resources: Padhna Kahan Se Hai?</strong></h2><p>7 din mein aap textbooks nahi padh sakte. Aapko chahiye \"Filtered Content.\"</p><ol><li><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in/notes\"><strong>MyCollegeVerse Handwritten Notes</strong></a><strong>:</strong> Humne toppers ke notes ko compress karke \"Exam-ready\" banaya hai.&nbsp;</li><li><strong>The \"Quantum\" Series:</strong> Iska use sirf questions identify karne ke liye karein, answer hamare notes se padhein (kyunki Quantum ki language thodi robotic hoti hai).</li><li><strong>YouTube One-Shot Videos:</strong> 10-12 ghante ke lectures mat dekhiye. Sirf 1-hour revision videos dekhiye.</li></ol><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>5. Avoiding the \"Carry Over\" (Back) Trap</strong></h2><p>AKTU mein back lagne ka sabse bada reason \"Empty Sheets\" ya \"Out of Context\" likhna hota hai.</p><ul><li><strong>Don\'t Leave Blanks:</strong> Agar question nahi aata, toh us topic se related jo bhi pata ho, wo likhein. Diagram banayein.</li><li><strong>Sequence Matters:</strong> Sabse pehle wo answers likhein jo aapko best aate hain (Best Impression). Section A ko end mein bhi kar sakte hain agar confidence kam ho.</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>6. Pro-Tips for Different Subjects</strong></h2><ul><li><strong>Theory Subjects (HVPE, Cyber Security):</strong> Inme examples aur real-life scenarios likhein.</li><li><strong>Numerical Subjects (Maths, DSTL):</strong> Steps saaf rakhein. Final answer ko box mein band karein.</li><li><strong>Coding Subjects (Python, C, Java):</strong> Dry run ya flowcharts zaroor banayein.</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>7. Conclusion: Can You Do It?</strong></h2><p>Yes, 7 din AKTU exams ke liye \"kafi se zyada\" hain agar aap smart work karein. Yaad rakhiye, aapka goal \"Scientist\" banna nahi, balki \"Engineer\" banke degree nikaalna hai.</p><p>Apne doston ke saath ye guide share karein aur padhna shuru karein. All the best!</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p>', 'Stuck with AKTU exams in 7 days? Learn the secret strategy to score 8+ SGPA using our proven 7-day sprint, answer sheet presentation tips, and curated notes', '/blogs/how-to-pass-aktu-exams-in-7-days-the-survival-guide-1778790321_tKK2iJlNC', 'How to Pass AKTU Exams in 7 Days: The Ultimate Survival Guide (2026 Edition)', 'How to Pass AKTU Exams in 7 Days: The Ultimate Survival Guide (2026 Edition)', 'Stuck with AKTU exams in 7 days? Learn the secret strategy to score 8+ SGPA using our proven 7-day sprint, answer sheet presentation tips, and curated notes', 'pass aktu exams, how to pass aktu exam in one night, aktu answer sheet writing tips, aktu exam strategy 2026,mycollegeverse handwritten notes', 70, 100, 0, '[]', 0, 0, NULL, '2026-05-14 20:25:24', '2026-05-14 20:25:24');

CREATE TABLE `blog_categories` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `description` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_active` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `blog_categories`
--

--
-- Table structure for table `chat_messages`
--

CREATE TABLE `chat_messages` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `sender_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `receiver_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `message` text NOT NULL,
  `type` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'text',
  `image_path` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_read` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `chat_messages`
--

(44, 1, 2, 'Congratulations Md Arsalan! Your candidacy for Part Time Public Speaking Tutor has been **Shortlisted**. We will be reaching out via this channel for the next synchronization steps. Great work!', 'text', NULL, 1, '2026-04-25 19:53:19', '2026-04-26 06:11:52'),
(45, 1, 2, '📅 **Interview Invitation** — Part Time Public Speaking Tutor at studycubs\n\n🗓️ **Date & Time:** Tue, 28 Apr 2026 • 11:40 AM\n🔗 **Meeting Link:** https://meet.goofle.com/xyxtye\n\nHi{Name}, we would like to invite you for the interview', 'text', NULL, 1, '2026-04-26 06:11:26', '2026-04-26 06:11:52'),
(46, 1, 3, '📅 **Interview Invitation** — Part Time Public Speaking Tutor at studycubs\n\n🗓️ **Date & Time:** Tue, 28 Apr 2026 • 11:40 AM\n🔗 **Meeting Link:** https://meet.goofle.com/xyxtye\n\nHi{Name}, we would like to invite you for the interview', 'text', NULL, 0, '2026-04-26 06:11:26', '2026-04-26 06:11:26'),
(47, 1, 3, '📅 **Interview Invitation** — Part Time Public Speaking Tutor at studycubs\n\n🗓️ **Date & Time:** Tue, 28 Apr 2026 • 11:43 AM\n🔗 **Meeting Link:** https://mycollegeverse.in\n\nhi{name}, you have been invite for next round', 'text', NULL, 0, '2026-04-26 06:14:14', '2026-04-26 06:14:14'),

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `colleges`
--

CREATE TABLE `colleges` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `type` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `streams` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`streams`)),
  `slug` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `description` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `location` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `state` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `city` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `thumbnail_url` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `student_count` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `rating` double(8,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 5.00,
  `tags` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`tags`)),
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `colleges`
--

INSERT INTO `colleges` (`id`, `name`, `type`, `streams`, `slug`, `description`, `location`, `state`, `city`, `thumbnail_url`, `student_count`, `rating`, `tags`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(5, 'VIT Vellore', NULL, NULL, 'vellore-institute-of-technology', 'Vellore Institute of Technology (VIT) is a premier private research university. It is known for its world-class infrastructure and a massive student community from across the globe.', 'Vellore, Tamil Nadu', NULL, NULL, 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585?q=80&w=1000', 35000, 4.60, '[\"Engineering Excellence\",\"Vibrant Campus\",\"Research\"]', '2026-04-09 07:14:46', '2026-04-09 12:35:54'),
(119, 'Noida International University', 'Private', '[\"Engineering\",\"MBA\",\"MBBS\"]', 'noida-international-university', 'Emerging university with global collaborations and diverse programs.', 'Yamuna Expressway', 'Uttar Pradesh', 'Greater Noida', 'https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/6/6c/Noida_International_University_logo.png', 2885, 0.00, '[\"Private\"]', '2026-04-14 19:05:05', '2026-04-14 19:05:05'),
(142, 'Indian Institute of Technology Delhi', 'Government', '[\"Delhi\"]', 'indian-institute-of-technology-delhi', 'India\'s premier engineering institute with global recognition and top-tier placements.', 'Hauz Khas, Delhi', 'New Delhi', 'Engineering, B.Tech, M.Tech', 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585', 0, 5.00, '[\"IIT\",\"Tier1\",\"Elite\"]', '2026-04-14 20:11:32', '2026-04-14 20:11:32'),
(143, 'All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi', 'Government', '[\"Delhi\"]', 'all-india-institute-of-medical-sciences-delhi', 'India\'s top medical institute known for excellence in healthcare and research.', 'Ansari Nagar, Delhi', 'New Delhi', 'MBBS', 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585', 0, 5.00, '[\"Medical\",\"Tier1\",\"Elite\"]', '2026-04-14 20:11:32', '2026-04-14 20:11:32'),
(144, 'University of Delhi', 'Central University', '[\"Delhi\"]', 'university-of-delhi', 'One of India\'s largest and most prestigious university systems.', 'Delhi', 'New Delhi', 'B.A, BSc, B.Com, MBA', 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585', 0, 5.00, '[\"DU\",\"Tier1\"]', '2026-04-14 20:11:32', '2026-04-14 20:11:32'),
(145, 'Jawaharlal Nehru University', 'Central University', '[\"Delhi\"]', 'jawaharlal-nehru-university', 'Globally recognized research university with strong academic reputation.', 'JNU Campus, Delhi', 'New Delhi', 'B.A, BSc, MBA', 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585', 0, 5.00, '[\"Research\",\"Elite\"]', '2026-04-14 20:11:32', '2026-04-14 20:11:32'),
(146, 'Jamia Millia Islamia', 'Central University', '[\"Delhi\"]', 'jamia-millia-islamia', 'Prestigious central university offering diverse programs and strong placements.', 'Jamia Nagar, Delhi', 'New Delhi', 'Engineering, B.A, BSc, MBA, B.Tech, M.Tech, Law', 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585', 0, 5.00, '[\"Central\",\"Tier1\"]', '2026-04-14 20:11:32', '2026-04-14 20:11:32'),
(147, 'Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University', 'State Government', '[\"Delhi\"]', 'guru-gobind-singh-indraprastha-university', 'Leading state university with strong technical and management programs.', 'Dwarka, Delhi', 'New Delhi', 'Engineering, MBA, BBA, Law', 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585', 0, 5.00, '[\"IPU\",\"Tier1\"]', '2026-04-14 20:11:32', '2026-04-14 20:11:32'),

(149, 'Delhi Technological University', 'State Government', '[\"Delhi\"]', 'delhi-technological-university', 'Top engineering university with excellent placements and alumni network.', 'Rohini, Delhi', 'New Delhi', 'Engineering, B.Tech, M.Tech, MBA', 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1562774053-701939374585', 0, 5.00, '[\"Engineering\",\"Tier1\"]', '2026-04-14 20:11:32', '2026-04-14 20:11:32'),

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `college_requests`
--

CREATE TABLE `college_requests` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `college_name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `city` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `state` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `student_email` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `message` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `status` enum('pending','approved','rejected') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `college_reviews`
--

CREATE TABLE `college_reviews` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `college_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `campus_rating` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `faculty_rating` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `academic_rating` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `comment` text NOT NULL,
  `reality_tags` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`reality_tags`)),
  `average_package` decimal(8,2) DEFAULT NULL,
  `lowest_package` decimal(8,2) DEFAULT NULL,
  `highest_package` decimal(8,2) DEFAULT NULL,
  `verification_id` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `status` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `college_reviews`
--

INSERT INTO `college_reviews` (`id`, `user_id`, `college_id`, `campus_rating`, `faculty_rating`, `academic_rating`, `comment`, `reality_tags`, `average_package`, `lowest_package`, `highest_package`, `verification_id`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(5, 2, 5, 2, 3, 2, 'campus environment is amazing and faculty is also okay okay', NULL, 5.00, 3.00, 25.80, 'PREVIOUSLY_VERIFIED', 'approved', '2026-04-14 09:48:57', '2026-04-14 09:49:18'),
(6, 9, 214, 5, 5, 5, 'school', '{\"canteen\":\"Survival Mode\",\"infra\":\"Standard\",\"attendance\":\"Moderate\",\"social\":\"Academic Focus\"}', 4.00, 2.50, 10.00, 'PREVIOUSLY_VERIFIED', 'approved', '2026-05-12 19:00:54', '2026-05-12 19:06:34');

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `comments`
--

CREATE TABLE `comments` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `parent_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `content` text NOT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `commentable_type` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `commentable_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Table structure for table `failed_jobs`
--

CREATE TABLE `failed_jobs` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `uuid` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `connection` text NOT NULL,
  `queue` text NOT NULL,
  `payload` longtext NOT NULL,
  `exception` longtext NOT NULL,
  `failed_at` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT current_timestamp()
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- Table structure for table `follows`
--

  `following_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,

) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Table structure for table `job_applications`
--

CREATE TABLE `job_applications` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `job_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `student_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `resume_path` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `resume_shared_link` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `about_me` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `why_hire` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `status` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_seen_by_student` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

INSERT INTO `job_applications` (`id`, `job_id`, `student_id`, `resume_path`, `resume_shared_link`, `about_me`, `why_hire`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `is_seen_by_student`) VALUES
(1, 2, 1, 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/4fef73f4-bd60-4799-a78f-4ab58eaa149c/', 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/4fef73f4-bd60-4799-a78f-4ab58eaa149c/', 'hehttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keys', 'https://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keyshttps://uploadcare.com/docs/start/settings/#keys', 'shortlisted', '2026-04-11 04:35:36', '2026-04-11 05:58:41', 1),

(3, 2, 2, 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/0b2b96d7-1d35-4536-b17e-7f6886ffdd60/', 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/0b2b96d7-1d35-4536-b17e-7f6886ffdd60/', 'this is arsalan with experince of more than 20 years in this domain', 'this is arsalan with experince of more than 20 years in this domain', 'pending', '2026-04-25 19:31:43', '2026-04-25 19:31:43', 1),
(4, 4, 2, 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/90bde3da-da4a-40d6-a11a-60ce17f5aba3/', 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/90bde3da-da4a-40d6-a11a-60ce17f5aba3/', 'hey this is md arsalan and applying for this role', 'hey this is md arsalan and applying for this role', 'shortlisted', '2026-04-25 19:52:25', '2026-04-25 19:53:19', 1),
(5, 4, 3, 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/76bc1ff4-47df-4b0e-816e-aaf2a53a8c20/', 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/76bc1ff4-47df-4b0e-816e-aaf2a53a8c20/', 'beacuse i need it and you have to hire me bhai karlo yr', 'hey bhai karlo hire yrr iase kaise kar rhe ho hai n', 'shortlisted', '2026-04-25 19:59:06', '2026-04-26 06:11:26', 1),
(6, 4, 5, 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/2bf5069b-ef0e-41e6-bc89-4845bd66521a/', 'https://5ya3oly4t3.ucarecd.net/2bf5069b-ef0e-41e6-bc89-4845bd66521a/', 'hey i think i fit for this role and i really want this job', 'hey i think i fit for this role and i really want this job', 'shortlisted', '2026-04-26 07:42:05', '2026-04-26 07:43:38', 1);

--
-- Table structure for table `job_postings`
--

CREATE TABLE `job_postings` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `recruiter_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `target_college_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `description` text NOT NULL,
  `type` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Full-time',
  `location` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `salary_range` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_approved` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `status` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `job_postings`
--

INSERT INTO `job_postings` (`id`, `recruiter_id`, `target_college_id`, `title`, `slug`, `description`, `type`, `location`, `salary_range`, `is_approved`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(1, 17, NULL, 'Senior Consultant', 'senior-consultant-1', 'JD:\r\nAbout the job\r\nJunior Data Analyst Intern – Infrabyte Consulting (Remote)\r\n\r\n\r\nLocation: Remote\r\nRole Type: Full-Time Internship\r\nStipend: ₹18,500/month\r\nDuration: 1–3 Months\r\n\r\n\r\nInfrabyte Consulting is offering an opportunity for aspiring data professionals to work closely with real datasets and business problems. As a Data Analyst Intern, you will not just work with numbers, but learn how to interpret data, extract insights, and contribute to smarter decision-making.\r\n\r\n\r\nRole Overview\r\nThis internship focuses on building practical data skills by working on live projects. You will gain exposure to how organizations collect, process, and use data to improve performance and strategy.\r\n\r\n\r\nYour Day-to-Day Work\r\n* Prepare and structure raw data for analysis\r\n* Check data quality and ensure consistency across datasets\r\n* Build reports and dashboards to present insights clearly\r\n* Identify meaningful patterns and trends in data\r\n* Assist in improving reporting systems and processes\r\n* Work with different teams to understand data requirements\r\n* Support ongoing analysis tasks and business reporting needs\r\n* Keep records of analysis methods and outputs\r\n\r\n\r\nSkills That Will Help You Succeed\r\n* Comfortable working with Excel or Google Sheets\r\n* Basic understanding of how data is analyzed and interpreted\r\n* Knowledge of SQL is helpful but not mandatory\r\n* Exposure to Python or similar tools is an added benefit\r\n* Strong logical thinking and attention to detail\r\n* Ability to communicate insights in a simple way\r\n* Willingness to learn and adapt in a remote work setup\r\n\r\n\r\nWho This Role Is For\r\n* Students or graduates exploring a career in data analytics\r\n* Freshers who want practical, hands-on experience\r\n* Individuals who have worked on small data projects or certifications\r\n* Candidates available for a full-time remote internship\r\n\r\n\r\nWhat You Will Get\r\n* ₹18,500 monthly stipend\r\n* Real project experience working with business data\r\n* Exposure to tools used in analytics and reporting\r\n* Internship completion certificate\r\n* A strong foundation to start a career in data analytics\r\n* A learning environment focused on practical skills\r\n\r\n\r\nThis internship is suited for those who want to move beyond theory and understand how data is actually used in real-world business scenarios.', 'Full-time', 'Remote', '500000', 1, 'active', '2026-04-11 01:36:30', '2026-04-26 08:39:55'),
(2, 17, NULL, 'Senior Consultant R', 'senior-consultant-r-2', 'About the job\r\nJunior Data Analyst Intern – Infrabyte Consulting (Remote)\r\n\r\n\r\nLocation: Remote\r\nRole Type: Full-Time Internship\r\nStipend: ₹18,500/month\r\nDuration: 1–3 Months\r\n\r\n\r\nInfrabyte Consulting is offering an opportunity for aspiring data professionals to work closely with real datasets and business problems. As a Data Analyst Intern, you will not just work with numbers, but learn how to interpret data, extract insights, and contribute to smarter decision-making.\r\n\r\n\r\nRole Overview\r\nThis internship focuses on building practical data skills by working on live projects. You will gain exposure to how organizations collect, process, and use data to improve performance and strategy.\r\n\r\n\r\nYour Day-to-Day Work\r\n* Prepare and structure raw data for analysis\r\n* Check data quality and ensure consistency across datasets\r\n* Build reports and dashboards to present insights clearly\r\n* Identify meaningful patterns and trends in data\r\n* Assist in improving reporting systems and processes\r\n* Work with different teams to understand data requirements\r\n* Support ongoing analysis tasks and business reporting needs\r\n* Keep records of analysis methods and outputs\r\n\r\n\r\nSkills That Will Help You Succeed\r\n* Comfortable working with Excel or Google Sheets\r\n* Basic understanding of how data is analyzed and interpreted\r\n* Knowledge of SQL is helpful but not mandatory\r\n* Exposure to Python or similar tools is an added benefit\r\n* Strong logical thinking and attention to detail\r\n* Ability to communicate insights in a simple way\r\n* Willingness to learn and adapt in a remote work setup\r\n\r\n\r\nWho This Role Is For\r\n* Students or graduates exploring a career in data analytics\r\n* Freshers who want practical, hands-on experience\r\n* Individuals who have worked on small data projects or certifications\r\n* Candidates available for a full-time remote internship\r\n\r\n\r\nWhat You Will Get\r\n* ₹18,500 monthly stipend\r\n* Real project experience working with business data\r\n* Exposure to tools used in analytics and reporting\r\n* Internship completion certificate\r\n* A strong foundation to start a career in data analytics\r\n* A learning environment focused on practical skills\r\n\r\n\r\nThis internship is suited for those who want to move beyond theory and understand how data is actually used in real-world business scenarios.', 'Full-time', 'Remote', '500000', 1, 'active', '2026-04-11 01:55:09', '2026-04-26 08:39:55'),
(3, 1, NULL, 'PART TIME TEACHER', 'part-time-teacher-3', 'Job Title: Part-Time English Public Speaking Tutor\r\n\r\nCompany: StudyCubs (studycubs.com)\r\nJob Type: Part-Time (Remote)\r\n\r\nAbout the Role:\r\nStudyCubs is looking for a passionate and engaging English Public Speaking Tutor to help students build confidence, fluency, and effective communication skills. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy teaching, mentoring, and working with learners in an interactive online environment.\r\n\r\nKey Responsibilities:\r\n\r\nConduct live online public speaking sessions focused on communication, confidence-building, and speech delivery\r\nGuide students in improving pronunciation, vocabulary, and presentation skills\r\nProvide constructive feedback and track student progress\r\nCreate a positive, supportive, and interactive learning environment\r\nFollow structured lesson plans while adapting to individual student needs\r\n\r\nClass Details:\r\n\r\nDuration: 1 hour 15 minutes per session\r\nFrequency: 12 classes per month\r\nMode: Online (remote)\r\n\r\nCompensation:\r\n\r\n₹250 per hour\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\nStrong command of spoken and written English\r\nGood public speaking and presentation skills\r\nPrior teaching/tutoring experience preferred (but not mandatory)\r\nComfortable using online teaching platforms\r\nPatient, enthusiastic, and student-focused approach\r\n\r\nPreferred Qualifications:\r\n\r\nBackground in English, Communication, Education, or related fields\r\nExperience in debate, theatre, or public speaking training is a plus\r\n\r\nWhy Join StudyCubs?\r\n\r\nFlexible part-time schedule\r\nOpportunity to impact students’ confidence and communication skills\r\nSupportive and collaborative teaching environment', 'Contract', 'Remote', '250 Per Hour', 1, 'active', '2026-04-25 19:35:42', '2026-04-26 08:39:55'),
(4, 1, NULL, 'Part Time Public Speaking Tutor', 'part-time-public-speaking-tutor-4', 'Job Title: Part-Time English Public Speaking Tutor\r\n\r\nCompany: StudyCubs (studycubs.com)\r\nJob Type: Part-Time (Remote)\r\n\r\nAbout the Role:\r\nStudyCubs is looking for a passionate and engaging English Public Speaking Tutor to help students build confidence, fluency, and effective communication skills. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy teaching, mentoring, and working with learners in an interactive online environment.\r\n\r\nKey Responsibilities:\r\n\r\nConduct live online public speaking sessions focused on communication, confidence-building, and speech delivery\r\nGuide students in improving pronunciation, vocabulary, and presentation skills\r\nProvide constructive feedback and track student progress\r\nCreate a positive, supportive, and interactive learning environment\r\nFollow structured lesson plans while adapting to individual student needs\r\n\r\nClass Details:\r\n\r\nDuration: 1 hour 15 minutes per session\r\nFrequency: 12 classes per month\r\nMode: Online (remote)\r\n\r\nCompensation:\r\n\r\n₹250 per hour\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\nStrong command of spoken and written English\r\nGood public speaking and presentation skills\r\nPrior teaching/tutoring experience preferred (but not mandatory)\r\nComfortable using online teaching platforms\r\nPatient, enthusiastic, and student-focused approach\r\n\r\nPreferred Qualifications:\r\n\r\nBackground in English, Communication, Education, or related fields\r\nExperience in debate, theatre, or public speaking training is a plus\r\n\r\nWhy Join StudyCubs?\r\n\r\nFlexible part-time schedule\r\nOpportunity to impact students’ confidence and communication skills\r\nSupportive and collaborative teaching environment', 'Contract', 'Remote', '250 Per hour', 1, 'active', '2026-04-25 19:40:35', '2026-04-26 08:39:55'),
(6, 1, NULL, 'maths 2', 'maths-2', 'test', 'Full-time', 'local', '250', 1, 'deleted', '2026-04-26 08:41:30', '2026-04-30 15:43:51'),
(7, 1, NULL, 'Content Writer Intern Unpaid + PPO – StudyCubs.com', 'content-writer-intern-unpaid-ppo-studycubscom', 'Location: Remote\r\nDuration: 3 Months (Unpaid Internship)\r\nPPO: Performance-Based (Full-Time Opportunity After Internship)\r\n\r\n🚀 About StudyCubs\r\n\r\nStudyCubs is a student-focused platform helping learners with academic resources, study materials, career guidance, and productivity content. We aim to simplify student life through valuable, easy-to-understand content.\r\n\r\n🎯 Role Overview\r\n\r\nWe are looking for passionate college students who are interested in content writing and want to build real-world experience. This is a beginner-friendly role—no prior professional experience required, just a willingness to learn and improve.\r\n\r\n✍️ Responsibilities\r\nResearch and write SEO-friendly articles/blogs related to:\r\nStudent life & productivity\r\nStudy tips & exam strategies\r\nCareer guidance & college insights\r\nCreate engaging, easy-to-read content for beginners\r\nFollow basic SEO guidelines (training will be provided)\r\nEdit and improve content based on feedback\r\nMaintain consistency in tone and quality\r\nMeet deadlines for assigned tasks\r\n\r\n Requirements\r\nCurrently pursuing a college degree (any stream)\r\nBasic understanding of English writing and grammar\r\nInterest in writing, blogging, or storytelling\r\nWillingness to learn SEO and content strategies\r\nAbility to do basic research using Google\r\nConsistency and commitment for 3 months\r\n\r\n What You’ll Gain\r\nHands-on experience in content writing & SEO\r\nOpportunity to build a strong writing portfolio\r\nMentorship and feedback to improve your skills\r\nExposure to real-world content and digital marketing workflows\r\nCertificate of completion\r\nPPO (Pre-Placement Offer) for top performers\r\n\r\n Who Should Apply?\r\nBeginners who want to start their journey in content writing\r\nStudents interested in digital marketing, blogging, or SEO\r\nAnyone looking to gain practical experience while studying\r\n\r\n Selection Process\r\nApplication submission\r\nShort writing task (evaluation round)\r\nFinal selection', 'Internship', 'Remote', 'Unpaid + PPO', 1, 'active', '2026-04-30 15:46:17', '2026-04-30 15:46:50');

CREATE TABLE `mentorship_requests` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `requester_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `mentor_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `subject` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `message` text NOT NULL,
  `status` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  `rating` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `feedback` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

(82, '2026_05_06_144719_create_assignment_submissions_table', 53),
(83, '2026_05_06_144721_create_assignment_evaluations_table', 53),
(84, '2026_05_06_145322_add_file_id_to_assignment_submissions_table', 53),
(85, '2026_05_06_152135_add_phone_to_assignment_submissions_table', 54),
(86, '2026_05_09_134700_create_academic_guides_table', 55),
(87, '2026_05_09_094225_add_file_path_to_academic_guides_table', 56),
(88, '2026_05_12_110646_add_alt_text_to_blogs_table', 56);

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `notes`
--

CREATE TABLE `notes` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `note_type` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pdf',
  `exam_name` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_pyq` tinyint(1) DEFAULT 0,
  `pyq_year` varchar(10) DEFAULT NULL,
  `ai_content` longtext DEFAULT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `file_path` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `subject_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED DEFAULT NULL,
  `custom_subject` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_verified` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `college_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `downloads` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `notes`
--

INSERT INTO `notes` (`id`, `title`, `note_type`, `exam_name`, `is_pyq`, `pyq_year`, `ai_content`, `slug`, `file_path`, `subject_id`, `custom_subject`, `is_verified`, `user_id`, `college_id`, `downloads`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(41, 'operating system notes aktu', 'pdf', NULL, 0, NULL, NULL, 'operating-system-notes-aktu', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1776275410/nqadpzzpqhulhonsph4v.pdf', NULL, 'Operating System', 1, 5, 5, 0, '2026-04-15 17:50:11', '2026-04-15 17:50:18'),
(42, 'combustion engine', 'ai', NULL, 0, NULL, '<h2>Introduction to Combustion Engines</h2>\n<p>The <strong>Internal Combustion (IC) Engine</strong> is the heart of modern mechanical engineering and transportation. By definition, an IC engine is a heat engine where the combustion of fuel occurs with an oxidizer (usually air) in a confined space called a combustion chamber. This exothermic reaction creates high-temperature and high-pressure gases, which expand and apply direct force to a component of the engine, such as a piston or turbine blades. This force moves the component over a distance, transforming <strong>chemical energy into thermal energy</strong>, and ultimately into <strong>mechanical work</strong>.</p>\n\n<p>In the study of Basic Mechanical Engineering, understanding the IC engine is fundamental because it integrates thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, and kinematics. Unlike External Combustion (EC) engines (like steam engines), where the working fluid is heated by a separate furnace, IC engines are compact, start quickly, and offer higher thermal efficiencies for mobile applications.</p>\n\n<h2>Classification of Heat Engines</h2>\n<p>Before diving into the specifics of IC engines, it is essential to categorize them within the broader scope of heat engines:</p>\n<ul>\n    <li><strong>External Combustion Engines (ECE):</strong> The products of combustion transfer heat to a separate working fluid. <em>Example: Steam turbines, Stirling engines.</em></li>\n    <li><strong>Internal Combustion Engines (ICE):</strong> The products of combustion act as the working fluid themselves. <em>Example: Petrol engines, Diesel engines, Gas turbines.</em></li>\n</ul>\n\n<h3>Main Classifications of IC Engines</h3>\n<ol>\n    <li><strong>According to Cycle of Operation:</strong> Otto Cycle (Petrol), Diesel Cycle (Diesel), and Dual Cycle.</li>\n    <li><strong>According to Number of Strokes:</strong> Two-stroke and Four-stroke engines.</li>\n    <li><strong>According to Method of Ignition:</strong> Spark Ignition (SI) and Compression Ignition (CI).</li>\n    <li><strong>According to Fuel Used:</strong> Petrol, Diesel, CNG, LPG, Hydrogen, or Bio-fuels.</li>\n    <li><strong>According to Cooling System:</strong> Air-cooled and Liquid-cooled.</li>\n    <li><strong>According to Cylinder Arrangement:</strong> Vertical, Horizontal, V-type, Radial, and Opposed-piston.</li>\n</ol>\n\n<h2>Engine Terminology: Key Definitions</h2>\n<p>To analyze engine performance, one must master the standard academic vocabulary used in mechanical workshops and laboratories:</p>\n<ul>\n    <li><strong>Bore (d):</strong> The inner diameter of the engine cylinder.</li>\n    <li><strong>Stroke (L):</strong> The nominal distance traveled by the piston between its two extreme positions.</li>\n    <li><strong>Dead Centers:</strong> The extreme positions of the piston. \n        <ul>\n            <li><em>Top Dead Center (TDC):</em> The position where the piston is farthest from the crankshaft.</li>\n            <li><em>Bottom Dead Center (BDC):</em> The position where the piston is closest to the crankshaft.</li>\n        </ul>\n    </li>\n    <li><strong>Swept Volume ($V_s$):</strong> Also known as displacement volume. It is the volume displaced by the piston as it moves from TDC to BDC. Formula: $V_s = (\\pi/4) \\times d^2 \\times L$.</li>\n    <li><strong>Clearance Volume ($V_c$):</strong> The volume of the combustion chamber when the piston is at TDC.</li>\n    <li><strong>Total Volume ($V_t$):</strong> The sum of swept volume and clearance volume ($V_s + V_c$).</li>\n    <li><strong>Compression Ratio (r):</strong> The ratio of the total volume to the clearance volume. It is a critical factor in determining engine efficiency.\n        <br><em>Formula: $r = (V_s + V_c) / V_c$</em>\n    </li>\n</ul>\n\n<h2>Major Components of an IC Engine</h2>\n<p>An IC engine is an assembly of several precision-engineered components working in synchronization. Here are the primary parts:</p>\n\n<h3>1. Cylinder Block and Cylinder Head</h3>\n<p>The <strong>cylinder block</strong> is the main structural body of the engine, usually made of cast iron or aluminum alloy. It contains the cylinders where the piston moves. The <strong>cylinder head</strong> closes the top of the cylinder and houses the spark plug or fuel injector, along with the intake and exhaust valves.</p>\n\n<h3>2. Piston and Piston Rings</h3>\n<p>The <strong>piston</strong> is a cylindrical component that reciprocates inside the cylinder. Its primary job is to transmit the force of expanding gases to the connecting rod. <strong>Piston rings</strong> are fitted into grooves on the piston to provide a seal (preventing gas leakage) and to scrape excess oil from the cylinder walls.</p>\n\n<h3>3. Connecting Rod and Crankshaft</h3>\n<p>The <strong>connecting rod</strong> acts as a bridge between the piston and the crankshaft. It converts the linear reciprocating motion of the piston into the rotational motion of the <strong>crankshaft</strong>. The crankshaft eventually drives the vehicle\'s wheels through the transmission system.</p>\n\n<h3>4. Valves and Camshaft</h3>\n<p>The <strong>intake valve</strong> allows the air-fuel mixture (in SI engines) or air (in CI engines) to enter the cylinder, while the <strong>exhaust valve</strong> permits the exit of burned gases. The <strong>camshaft</strong>, driven by the crankshaft, controls the timing and opening/closing of these valves.</p>\n\n<h2>Working Principle: The Four-Stroke Cycle</h2>\n<p>Most modern passenger vehicles operate on the four-stroke cycle, which completes one power cycle over two revolutions of the crankshaft (720 degrees). The mnemonic <strong>\"Suck, Squeeze, Bang, Blow\"</strong> is a helpful way for students to remember these stages.</p>\n\n<h3>Step 1: Suction (Intake) Stroke</h3>\n<p>The piston moves from TDC to BDC. The intake valve is open, and the exhaust valve is closed. The downward movement of the piston creates a vacuum, drawing in the charge (air-fuel mixture in petrol engines; pure air in diesel engines).</p>\n\n<h3>Step 2: Compression Stroke</h3>\n<p>Both valves are closed. The piston moves from BDC to TDC. The charge is compressed into the clearance volume, significantly increasing its pressure and temperature. In SI engines, the spark plug ignites the mixture near the end of this stroke. In CI engines, fuel is injected and ignites due to high heat.</p>\n\n<h3>Step 3: Power (Expansion) Stroke</h3>\n<p>The high-pressure gases resulting from combustion push the piston down from TDC to BDC. This is the only stroke where <strong>useful mechanical work</strong> is produced. Both valves remain closed during the initial part of this stroke.</p>\n\n<h3>Step 4: Exhaust Stroke</h3>\n<p>The exhaust valve opens while the intake valve remains closed. The piston moves from BDC to TDC, pushing the spent combustion products out of the cylinder into the exhaust manifold.</p>\n\n<h2>Comparison: SI vs. CI Engines</h2>\n<p>Understanding the distinction between Spark Ignition (SI) and Compression Ignition (CI) engines is a common examination topic.</p>\n\n<h3>Spark Ignition (SI) Engines</h3>\n<ul>\n    <li><strong>Cycle:</strong> Works on the Otto Cycle.</li>\n    <li><strong>Fuel:</strong> Highly volatile (Petrol/Gasoline).</li>\n    <li><strong>Introduction of Fuel:</strong> A mixture of air and fuel is prepared in the carburetor or fuel injection system before entering the cylinder.</li>\n    <li><strong>Ignition:</strong> Requires a spark plug to initiate combustion.</li>\n    <li><strong>Compression Ratio:</strong> Typically lower (6:1 to 10:1) to prevent \"knocking\" or pre-ignition.</li>\n</ul>\n\n<h3>Compression Ignition (CI) Engines</h3>\n<ul>\n    <li><strong>Cycle:</strong> Works on the Diesel Cycle.</li>\n    <li><strong>Fuel:</strong> Less volatile (Diesel).</li>\n    <li><strong>Introduction of Fuel:</strong> Only air is compressed. Fuel is injected directly into the combustion chamber at the end of the compression stroke.</li>\n    <li><strong>Ignition:</strong> Self-ignition occurs because the air temperature exceeds the fuel\'s self-ignition temperature.</li>\n    <li><strong>Compression Ratio:</strong> Much higher (16:1 to 24:1), leading to higher thermal efficiency.</li>\n</ul>\n\n<h2>The Two-Stroke Engine: A Simplified Alternative</h2>\n<p>In a two-stroke engine, the entire cycle (Suction, Compression, Power, Exhaust) is completed in just <strong>one revolution</strong> of the crankshaft (two strokes of the piston). Instead of valves, two-stroke engines typically use <strong>ports</strong> (intake, exhaust, and transfer ports) in the cylinder wall.</p>\n<p><em>Practical Example:</em> Small engines for chainsaws, lawnmowers, and older mopeds often use two-stroke designs because they are lighter and have a higher power-to-weight ratio, though they are generally less fuel-efficient and more polluting than four-stroke engines.</p>\n\n<h2>Engine Performance Parameters</h2>\n<p>As an engineer, you must quantify how well an engine performs using the following metrics:</p>\n\n<h3>1. Indicated Power (IP)</h3>\n<p>The total power developed inside the engine cylinder by the combustion of fuel. It does not account for internal friction.</p>\n\n<h3>2. Brake Power (BP)</h3>\n<p>The actual power available at the crankshaft for doing useful work. It is measured using a dynamometer.\n<br><em>BP = IP - Friction Power (FP)</em></p>\n\n<h3>3. Mechanical Efficiency ($\\eta_m$)</h3>\n<p>The ratio of Brake Power to Indicated Power. It indicates how much power is lost to internal friction.\n<br><em>$\\eta_m = (BP / IP) \\times 100\\%$</em></p>\n\n<h3>4. Specific Fuel Consumption (SFC)</h3>\n<p>The mass of fuel consumed per unit of power produced per hour.
