Lower SFC indicates a more economical engine.</p>\n\n<h2>Cooling and Lubrication Systems</h2>\n<p>Without support systems, an IC engine would destroy itself within minutes due to heat and friction.</p>\n\n<h3>Cooling Systems</h3>\n<p>Only about 30% of the energy from fuel is converted into useful work; the rest is heat. \n<ul>\n    <li><strong>Air Cooling:</strong> Uses fins on the cylinder head and block to dissipate heat into the atmosphere. Common in motorcycles.</li>\n    <li><strong>Liquid Cooling:</strong> Circulates a coolant (water + antifreeze) through jackets around the cylinders. The heat is then dissipated via a radiator. Standard in cars and trucks.</li>\n</ul>\n</p>\n\n<h3>Lubrication Systems</h3>\n<p>Lubrication reduces friction between moving parts, cools components, and cleans the engine by carrying away debris. Methods include:\n<ul>\n    <li><strong>Mist Lubrication:</strong> Used in 2-stroke engines where oil is mixed with fuel.</li>\n    <li><strong>Splash Lubrication:</strong> The connecting rod \"splashes\" into an oil sump.</li>\n    <li><strong>Pressure Lubrication:</strong> An oil pump forces lubricant to all critical bearings.</li>\n</ul>\n</p>\n\n<h2>Modern Advancements in Combustion Engines</h2>\n<p>To meet environmental regulations and consumer demand for power, modern engineering has introduced several innovations:</p>\n<ul>\n    <li><strong>Turbocharging and Supercharging:</strong> These systems force more air into the combustion chamber, allowing more fuel to be burned and increasing power without increasing engine size.</li>\n    <li><strong>Variable Valve Timing (VVT):</strong> Adjusts the timing of valve opening to optimize efficiency and torque across different engine speeds.</li>\n    <li><strong>Direct Injection:</strong> Injecting fuel directly into the combustion chamber at very high pressures for better atomization and cleaner burning.</li>\n    <li><strong>Hybridization:</strong> Combining an IC engine with an electric motor to capture energy lost during braking and improve overall fuel economy.</li>\n</ul>\n\n<h2>Key Takeaways</h2>\n<div style=\"background-color: #f9f9f9; padding: 20px; border-left: 5px solid #2c3e50;\">\n    <ul>\n        <li><strong>IC Engines</strong> convert chemical energy into mechanical work through internal combustion.</li>\n        <li>The <strong>Compression Ratio</strong> is a fundamental design parameter; higher ratios generally lead to better efficiency but require stronger materials and specific fuels.</li>\n        <li>The <strong>Four-Stroke Cycle</strong> consists of Intake, Compression, Power, and Exhaust. <strong>Power</strong> is the only stroke producing work.</li>\n        <li><strong>SI Engines</strong> use spark plugs and petrol, while <strong>CI Engines</strong> use high compression and diesel.</li>\n        <li><strong>Brake Power</strong> is the usable power at the crankshaft, whereas <strong>Indicated Power</strong> is the theoretical power generated in the cylinder.</li>\n        <li>Effective <strong>Cooling and Lubrication</strong> are vital for engine longevity and preventing catastrophic thermal failure.</li>\n    </ul>\n</div>', 'combustion-engine', NULL, 16, NULL, 1, 5, 5, 0, '2026-04-17 12:23:52', '2026-04-17 12:23:52'),
(43, 'Ray Optics', 'ai', NULL, 0, NULL, '<article>\n    <header>\n        <h1>Study Notes: Ray Optics (Geometrical Optics)</h1>\n        <p>Ray Optics deals with the study of light as it travels in straight lines (rays). It is an approximation valid when the size of the obstacle or aperture is much larger than the wavelength of light.</p>\n    </header>\n\n    <section>\n        <h2>Quick Revision Table</h2>\n        <table>\n            <thead>\n                <tr>\n                    <th>Parameter</th>\n                    <th>Spherical Mirrors</th>\n                    <th>Thin Lenses</th>\n                </tr>\n            </thead>\n            <tbody>\n                <tr>\n                    <td><strong>Fundamental Formula</strong></td>\n                    <td>1/v + 1/u = 1/f</td>\n                    <td>1/v - 1/u = 1/f</td>\n                </tr>\n                <tr>\n                    <td><strong>Magnification (m)</strong></td>\n                    <td>-v/u</td>\n                    <td>v/u</td>\n                </tr>\n                <tr>\n                    <td><strong>Focal Length (f)</strong></td>\n                    <td>R/2</td>\n                    <td>(n-1)(1/R₁ - 1/R₂)</td>\n                </tr>\n                <tr>\n                    <td><strong>Power (P)</strong></td>\n                    <td>N/A</td>\n                    <td>1/f (in meters)</td>\n                </tr>\n            </tbody>\n        </table>\n    </section>\n\n    <section>\n        <h2>1. Reflection of Light</h2>\n        <p>Reflection occurs when a light ray strikes a boundary and returns to the medium of origin. The <strong>Laws of Reflection</strong> state that the angle of incidence (i) equals the angle of reflection (r), and the incident ray, reflected ray, and normal all lie in the same plane.</p>\n\n        <div class=\'info-box\'>\n            <strong>Sign Convention (New Cartesian):</strong> \n            All distances are measured from the Pole (mirrors) or Optical Center (lenses). Distances in the direction of incident light are positive; opposite are negative.\n        </div>\n\n        <h3>Spherical Mirrors</h3>\n        <ul>\n            <li><strong>Concave Mirror:</strong> Converging in nature. Can form real (inverted) or virtual (erect) images depending on object position.</li>\n            <li><strong>Convex Mirror:</strong> Diverging in nature. Always forms a <strong>virtual, erect, and diminished</strong> image.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <div class=\'diagram-box\'>\n            [Diagram Concept: Ray diagram for a concave mirror with the object placed between C and F, showing the formation of an inverted, magnified real image beyond C.]\n        </div>\n    </section>\n\n    <section>\n        <h2>2. Refraction and Snell\'s Law</h2>\n        <p>Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one transparent medium to another due to a change in <strong>optical density</strong> (speed of light).</p>\n\n        <h3>Snell\'s Law</h3>\n        <p>For a given pair of media, the ratio of the sine of the angle of incidence to the sine of the angle of refraction is constant: <strong>n₁ sin i = n₂ sin r</strong>.</p>\n\n        <div class=\'exam-tip\'>\n            <strong>Mnemonic:</strong> Remember \"SRA\" (Slower to Rarer: Away). When light moves from a denser (slower) medium to a rarer (faster) medium, it bends <strong>Away</strong> from the normal.\n        </div>\n\n        <h3>Total Internal Reflection (TIR)</h3>\n        <p>TIR occurs when light travels from a <strong>denser</strong> to a <strong>rarer</strong> medium and the angle of incidence exceeds the <strong>Critical Angle (C)</strong>.</p>\n        <ul>\n            <li><strong>Critical Angle Formula:</strong> sin C = n₂ / n₁ (where n₁ > n₂).</li>\n            <li><strong>Applications:</strong> Optical fibers, mirages, and the brilliance of diamonds.</li>\n        </ul>\n    </section>\n\n    <section>\n        <h2>3. Lenses and Optical Power</h2>\n        <p>Lenses are transparent refracting media bounded by two surfaces, at least one of which is spherical.</p>\n\n        <h3>Lens Maker\'s Formula</h3>\n        <p>This is crucial for exam derivations. It relates the focal length to the refractive index of the material and the radii of curvature of the two surfaces: \n        <strong>1/f = (n - 1) [ (1/R₁) - (1/R₂) ]</strong>.</p>\n\n        <div class=\'info-box\'>\n            <strong>Power of a Lens:</strong> Measured in <strong>Diopters (D)</strong>. P = 1/f (f in meters). For a combination of thin lenses in contact, the total power is P = P₁ + P₂ + ...\n        </div>\n\n        <div class=\'diagram-box\'>\n            [Diagram Concept: A convex lens focusing parallel rays to a single focal point (F), and a concave lens diverging parallel rays so they appear to originate from F.]\n        </div>\n    </section>\n\n    <section>\n        <h2>4. Key Exam Focal Points</h2>\n        <ul>\n            <li><strong>Lateral Shift:</strong> The perpendicular distance between the incident ray and emergent ray in a glass slab.</li>\n            <li><strong>Normal Shift:</strong> The apparent displacement of an object placed in a denser medium when viewed from a rarer medium. <strong>Apparent Depth = Real Depth / n</strong>.</li>\n            <li><strong>Lens Displacements:</strong> If a lens is moved, the magnification changes. Remember that for real images, <strong>m is negative</strong>.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <div class=\'exam-tip\'>\n            <strong>Common Error:</strong> Always convert focal length to <strong>meters</strong> before calculating Power. Using cm is the most frequent cause of lost marks in competitive exams!\n        </div>\n    </section>\n</article>', 'ray-optics', NULL, 5, NULL, 1, 5, 5, 0, '2026-04-17 13:15:31', '2026-04-17 13:15:31'),
(44, 'working of window ac', 'ai', NULL, 0, NULL, '<section>\n    <h2>Quick Revision Table: Window AC Components</h2>\n    <table border=\"1\">\n        <thead>\n            <tr>\n                <th>Component</th>\n                <th>Location</th>\n                <th>Phase of Refrigerant</th>\n                <th>Primary Function</th>\n            </tr>\n        </thead>\n        <tbody>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Compressor</strong></td>\n                <td>Outdoor Side</td>\n                <td>Vapor (Low P to High P)</td>\n                <td>Increase pressure and temperature.</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Condenser</strong></td>\n                <td>Outdoor Side</td>\n                <td>Vapor to Liquid</td>\n                <td>Rejects heat to the outside air.</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Expansion Valve</strong></td>\n                <td>Middle Section</td>\n                <td>High P to Low P Liquid</td>\n                <td>Reduces pressure; causes cooling.</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Evaporator</strong></td>\n                <td>Indoor Side</td>\n                <td>Liquid to Vapor</td>\n                <td>Absorbs heat from the room air.</td>\n            </tr>\n        </tbody>\n    </table>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>Introduction to Window AC</h2>\n    <p>A <strong>Window Air Conditioner</strong> is a unitary refrigeration system designed to fit into a window sill or a hole in the wall. It operates on the <strong>Vapor Compression Refrigeration Cycle (VCRC)</strong> to maintain comfortable temperature and humidity levels within a confined space.</p>\n\n    <div class=\'info-box\'>\n        <strong>Definition:</strong> The VCRC is a thermodynamic cycle where a working fluid (refrigerant) undergoes phase changes to move heat from a low-temperature reservoir (room) to a high-temperature reservoir (atmosphere).\n    </div>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>Working Mechanism: The 4-Stage Cycle</h2>\n    <p>The operation of a window AC can be broken down into four distinct thermodynamic processes:</p>\n    \n    <pre class=\'mermaid\'>\n    graph TD\n        A[Compressor: High P, High T Vapor] --> B[Condenser: Heat Rejection]\n        B --> C[Expansion Valve: Pressure Drop]\n        C --> D[Evaporator: Heat Absorption]\n        D --> A\n        style A fill:#f96,stroke:#333\n        style B fill:#f66,stroke:#333\n        style C fill:#6f6,stroke:#333\n        style D fill:#6cf,stroke:#333\n    </pre>\n\n    <h3>1. Compression Stage</h3>\n    <ul>\n        <li>The <strong>Compressor</strong> sucks in low-pressure, low-temperature refrigerant vapor from the evaporator.</li>\n        <li>It compresses it into a <strong>high-pressure, high-temperature vapor</strong>.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>2. Condensation Stage</h3>\n    <ul>\n        <li>The hot vapor passes through the <strong>Condenser coils</strong> (located outside).</li>\n        <li>An outdoor fan blows air over these coils, causing the refrigerant to lose heat and condense into a <strong>high-pressure liquid</strong>.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>3. Expansion Stage</h3>\n    <ul>\n        <li>The liquid refrigerant passes through a <strong>Capillary Tube</strong> or Expansion Valve.</li>\n        <li>The sudden drop in pressure causes the refrigerant temperature to plummet, turning it into a <strong>cold, low-pressure liquid/vapor mix</strong>.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <h3>4. Evaporation Stage</h3>\n    <ul>\n        <li>The cold refrigerant enters the <strong>Evaporator coils</strong> (located inside).</li>\n        <li>The indoor blower pulls warm room air over these coils. The refrigerant <strong>absorbs heat</strong> from the air, cools the room, and evaporates back into a vapor to repeat the cycle.</li>\n    </ul>\n</section>\n\n<div class=\'exam-tip\'>\n    <strong>Professor\'s Exam Tip:</strong> Remember the mnemonic <strong>\"C-C-E-E\"</strong> (Compressor, Condenser, Expansion, Evaporator). In exams, always draw the distinction between the <strong>High-Pressure Side</strong> (Compressor/Condenser) and the <strong>Low-Pressure Side</strong> (Expansion/Evaporator). Mentioning <strong>Latent Heat</strong> during phase changes will gain you extra marks!\n</div>', 'working-of-window-ac', NULL, 16, NULL, 1, 4, 15, 0, '2026-04-17 13:56:52', '2026-04-17 13:56:52'),
(45, 'future of MERN', 'ai', NULL, 0, NULL, '<section>\n    <h2>Quick Revision Table: Future Trends in MERN Stack</h2>\n    <table>\n        <thead>\n            <tr>\n                <th>Component</th>\n                <th>Traditional Role</th>\n                <th>Future Evolution (Trend)</th>\n            </tr>\n        </thead>\n        <tbody>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>MongoDB</strong></td>\n                <td>NoSQL Document Store</td>\n                <td>Serverless Databases & Vector Search for AI (Atlas Vector Search)</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Express.js</strong></td>\n                <td>Node.js Web Framework</td>\n                <td>Integration with Fastify/Hono for higher performance and Type-safety</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>React</strong></td>\n                <td>Client-side UI Library</td>\n                <td>React Server Components (RSC) & Frameworks like Next.js/Remix</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Node.js</strong></td>\n                <td>Runtime Environment</td>\n                <td>Competition/Synergy with Bun and Deno; Native TypeScript support</td>\n            </tr>\n        </tbody>\n    </table>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>The Evolution of the MERN Ecosystem</h2>\n    <p>The <strong>MERN stack</strong> (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js) has dominated the web development landscape for years. However, as we move deeper into the 2020s, the stack is shifting from a \"collection of libraries\" to a highly integrated, performance-oriented ecosystem. The future of MERN is defined by <strong>Server-Side Rendering (SSR)</strong>, <strong>TypeScript ubiquity</strong>, and <strong>AI integration</strong>.</p>\n\n    <div class=\'info-box\'>\n        <strong>Definition: Full Stack Evolution</strong> - The transition of web development from monolithic architectures to modular, edge-computing-ready frameworks that prioritize developer experience (DX) and user performance.\n    </div>\n\n    <h3>1. The Shift to React Server Components (RSC)</h3>\n    <p>The \"R\" in MERN is no longer just about client-side rendering. Future MERN applications are leveraging <strong>React Server Components</strong>. This allows developers to render components on the server, reducing the JavaScript bundle size sent to the client and improving SEO and initial load times.</p>\n\n    <pre class=\'mermaid\'>\n    graph LR\n        A[Client Browser] -->|Requests Page| B(Next.js/React Server)\n        B -->|Fetch Data| C[(MongoDB Atlas)]\n        B -->|Render Server Components| D[Static HTML + Hydration Data]\n        D --> A\n    </pre>\n\n    <h3>2. The Rise of TypeScript and Type-Safety</h3>\n    <p>The industry is moving away from plain JavaScript towards <strong>TypeScript</strong>. The future of MERN involves \"End-to-End Type Safety,\" where the database schema (MongoDB), the backend API (Node/Express), and the frontend UI (React) share types, minimizing runtime errors and enhancing IDE support.</p>\n\n    <h3>3. Database Modernization: AI and Serverless</h3>\n    <p>MongoDB is evolving into more than just a document store. With the rise of Large Language Models (LLMs), <strong>MongoDB Atlas Vector Search</strong> is becoming a core part of the MERN stack, enabling developers to build AI-powered applications (like recommendation engines or chatbots) directly within the stack.</p>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>Technical Comparison: Traditional vs. Modern MERN</h2>\n    <table>\n        <thead>\n            <tr>\n                <th>Feature</th>\n                <th>Traditional MERN</th>\n                <th>Modern/Future MERN</th>\n            </tr>\n        </thead>\n        <tbody>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Data Fetching</strong></td>\n                <td>useEffect / Axios in React</td>\n                <td>Server Actions / SWR / React Query</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Language</strong></td>\n                <td>JavaScript (ES6+)</td>\n                <td>TypeScript (Strict Mode)</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Deployment</strong></td>\n                <td>Heroku / AWS EC2 (PaaS)</td>\n                <td>Vercel / Netlify / Edge Functions</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>State Management</strong></td>\n                <td>Redux / Context API</td>\n                <td>Zustand / Atomic State (Jotai)</td>\n            </tr>\n        </tbody>\n    </table>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h3>4. The Performance Revolution: Bun and Deno</h3>\n    <p>While <strong>Node.js</strong> remains the king, the entry of <strong>Bun</strong> and <strong>Deno</strong> is forcing the MERN ecosystem to prioritize speed. These runtimes offer native TypeScript support, faster package management, and built-in testing tools, which may eventually lead to a \"MERB\" (Bun) or \"MERD\" (Deno) variation in high-performance niches.</p>\n\n    <div class=\'exam-tip\'>\n        <strong>Mnemonic: \"S-A-T\" for Modern MERN</strong>\n        <ul>\n            <li><strong>S</strong>erver-side: Moving logic to the server (RSC).</li>\n            <li><strong>A</strong>I-Integrated: Using Vector databases.</li>\n            <li><strong>T</strong>ype-safe: Using TypeScript across the full stack.</li>\n        </ul>\n    </div>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>Conclusion: The Path Forward</h2>\n    <p>The future of MERN is not about replacing the core technologies, but about <strong>abstraction and optimization</strong>. Frameworks like <strong>Next.js</strong> effectively wrap the MERN stack into a more cohesive unit. Students and developers should focus on mastering <strong>Asynchronous patterns</strong>, <strong>Edge computing principles</strong>, and <strong>Cloud-native database management</strong> to stay relevant in the evolving job market.</p>\n</section>', 'future-of-mern', NULL, 41, NULL, 1, 3, 1, 0, '2026-04-17 15:07:41', '2026-04-17 15:07:41'),
(48, 'Complete AAI ATC Physics Syllabus 2025', 'competitive', 'AAI ATC', 0, NULL, NULL, 'complete-aai-atc-physics-syllabus-2025', 'https://visionary-blini-879154.netlify.app/aai%20atc%20phy%20syllabus.html', NULL, 'AAI ATC PHYSICS SYLLABUS', 1, 2, 2, 0, '2026-04-25 19:04:39', '2026-04-25 19:07:37'),
(49, 'AAI ATC Important Formula Booklet Physics', 'competitive', 'AAI ATC', 0, NULL, NULL, 'aai-atc-important-formula-booklet-physics', 'https://visionary-blini-879154.netlify.app/aai_atc_physics_formula_booklet_2025.html', NULL, 'AAI ATC Important Formula Booklet Physics', 1, 2, 2, 0, '2026-04-25 19:06:23', '2026-04-25 19:07:34'),
(50, 'Laws Of Motion Imp Notes', 'competitive', 'AAI ATC', 0, NULL, NULL, 'laws-of-motion-imp-notes', 'https://drive.google.com/file/d/11pxpKiHdisH3yCQiXwfSfx0EPBPLZQz9/view', NULL, 'AAI ATC PHYSICS', 1, 2, 2, 0, '2026-04-25 19:14:30', '2026-04-25 19:15:16'),
(51, 'sourcing management', 'ai', NULL, 0, NULL, '<section>\n    <h2>Quick Revision Table: Sourcing Management</h2>\n    <table>\n        <thead>\n            <tr>\n                <th>Concept</th>\n                <th>Primary Objective</th>\n                <th>Key Decision Factor</th>\n            </tr>\n        </thead>\n        <tbody>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Strategic Sourcing</strong></td>\n                <td>Long-term value & TCO</td>\n                <td>Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Outsourcing</strong></td>\n                <td>Focus on core competencies</td>\n                <td>Transaction cost & Capability</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Kraljic Matrix</strong></td>\n                <td>Risk mitigation & Profit impact</td>\n                <td>Supply risk vs. Profit impact</td>\n            </tr>\n        </tbody>\n    </table>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>Core Overview</h2>\n    <div class=\'info-box\'>\n        <strong>Sourcing Management</strong> is the strategic process of identifying, evaluating, and managing suppliers to acquire goods and services that provide the highest value to the organization, balancing cost, quality, and supply chain resilience.\n    </div>\n\n    <h3>The Strategic Sourcing Process</h3>\n    <p>Modern sourcing is a cyclical process rather than a one-time purchase. It involves shifting from a <strong>reactive procurement</strong> mindset to a <strong>proactive supply management</strong> approach.</p>\n    \n    <pre class=\'mermaid\'>\n    graph LR\n        A[Assessment of Needs] --> B[Market Research]\n        B --> C[RFP/RFQ Issuance]\n        C --> D[Supplier Selection]\n        D --> E[Contract Negotiation]\n        E --> F[Performance Monitoring]\n        F --> A\n    </pre>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>Strategic Tools: The Kraljic Matrix</h2>\n    <p>Used to classify suppliers into four categories based on <strong>supply risk</strong> and <strong>profit impact</strong>, dictating the procurement strategy.</p>\n    \n    <table>\n        <thead>\n            <tr>\n                <th>Quadrant</th>\n                <th>Description</th>\n                <th>Strategy</th>\n            </tr>\n        </thead>\n        <tbody>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Strategic</strong></td>\n                <td>High risk, High impact (e.g., core engines)</td>\n                <td>Partnership & Collaboration</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Leverage</strong></td>\n                <td>Low risk, High impact (e.g., bulk materials)</td>\n                <td>Competitive bidding/Tendering</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Bottleneck</strong></td>\n                <td>High risk, Low impact (e.g., specialized parts)</td>\n                <td>Ensure volume & Continuity</td>\n            </tr>\n            <tr>\n                <td><strong>Non-Critical</strong></td>\n                <td>Low risk, Low impact (e.g., office supplies)</td>\n                <td>Process efficiency/E-procurement</td>\n            </tr>\n        </tbody>\n    </table>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h3>Sourcing Strategies: Single vs. Multiple</h3>\n    <ul>\n        <li><strong>Single Sourcing:</strong> High dependency but enables <strong>economies of scale</strong> and deeper partnerships.</li>\n        <li><strong>Multiple Sourcing:</strong> Increases competition and reduces the <strong>risk of disruption</strong> (resilience).</li>\n        <li><strong>Global Sourcing:</strong> Accesses lower labor costs but increases <strong>logistics lead times</strong> and geopolitical risk.</li>\n    </ul>\n\n    <div class=\'exam-tip\'>\n        <strong>Mnemonic: T.C.O. (Total Cost of Ownership)</strong>. In exams, never just talk about \"price.\" Always mention TCO, which includes <strong>Acquisition</strong>, <strong>Ownership</strong> (maintenance/storage), and <strong>Post-Ownership</strong> (disposal) costs.\n    </div>\n</section>\n\n<section>\n    <h2>Performance Management</h2>\n    <p>Effective sourcing concludes with <strong>Supplier Relationship Management (SRM)</strong>. Key metrics (KPIs) include:</p>\n    <ul>\n        <li><strong>OTIF:</strong> On-Time In-Full delivery rate.</li>\n        <li><strong>Quality Defect Rate:</strong> Percentage of non-conforming goods.</li>\n        <li><strong>Lead Time Variability:</strong> Consistency of delivery speed.</li>\n    </ul>\n</section>', 'sourcing-management', NULL, NULL, 'operations', 1, 4, 15, 0, '2026-04-27 12:49:10', '2026-04-27 12:49:10'),
(55, 'QUALITY MANAGEMENT MBA AKTU NOTES', 'academic', NULL, 0, NULL, NULL, 'quality-management-mba-aktu-notes', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1777381988/iyhwre4mljidoiqqrces.pdf', NULL, 'Quality Management', 1, 7, 169, 0, '2026-04-28 13:13:09', '2026-04-28 13:13:15'),
(56, 'AKTU B.Tech  2ND YEAR CSE/IT Detailed Syllabus', 'academic', NULL, 0, NULL, NULL, 'aktu-btech-2nd-year-cseit-detailed-syllabus', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778313429/qdxia5kuiuglcpcxssb8.pdf', NULL, 'Syllabus', 1, 5, 5, 0, '2026-05-09 07:57:10', '2026-05-11 06:19:26'),
(58, 'Operating System Storage Architecture: From Physical Hardware to Data Abstraction', 'academic', NULL, 0, NULL, NULL, 'operating-system-storage-architecture-from-physical-hardware-to-data-abstraction', 'https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QGvy_rAw9zg0kfjBMBQhIYhClGK3qgH4/view?usp=sharing', 25, NULL, 1, 5, 5, 0, '2026-05-09 18:55:12', '2026-05-11 05:49:13'),
(71, 'JMI CET B.Sc Computer Science Previous Year Question Paper PDF 2026 | B65 Paper', 'competitive', 'JMI B.Sc CS Entrance Exam', 0, NULL, NULL, 'jmi-cet-bsc-computer-science-previous-year-question-paper-pdf-2026-b65-paper-2', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1778478467/kbe7xr18i97ndrazvhtz.pdf', NULL, 'JMI B.Sc CS Entrance Exam', 1, 8, 146, 0, '2026-05-11 05:47:47', '2026-05-11 05:49:09');
INSERT INTO `notes` (`id`, `title`, `note_type`, `exam_name`, `is_pyq`, `pyq_year`, `ai_content`, `slug`, `file_path`, `subject_id`, `custom_subject`, `is_verified`, `user_id`, `college_id`, `downloads`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(72, 'PPS AKTU NOTES', 'academic', NULL, 0, NULL, '<!DOCTYPE html>\r\n<html lang=\"en\">\r\n<head>\r\n  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />\r\n  <title>Programming for Problem Solving (PPS) – AKTU B.Tech 1st Year Smart Prep | MyCollegeVerse</title>\r\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1\" />\r\n\r\n  <meta name=\"description\" content=\"PPS (Programming for Problem Solving in C) smart prep notes for AKTU B.Tech 1st year. Covers loops, arrays, functions, pointers, DMA, file handling and most asked programs from last 5–6 years.\" />\r\n\r\n  <link href=\"https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Poppins:wght@500;700&family=Lato:wght@300;400;700&display=swap\" rel=\"stylesheet\" />\r\n\r\n  <style>\r\n    :root {\r\n      --primary: #1d3557;\r\n      --accent: #00b4d8;\r\n      --accent-soft: #edf2ff;\r\n      --bg: #f7f9fc;\r\n      --text: #1f2933;\r\n      --muted: #6b7b8d;\r\n      --card-bg: #ffffff;\r\n      --radius-lg: 18px;\r\n      --radius-sm: 10px;\r\n      --shadow-soft: 0 10px 30px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.12);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    * {\r\n      box-sizing: border-box;\r\n      margin: 0;\r\n      padding: 0;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    body {\r\n      font-family: \"Lato\", system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, \"Segoe UI\", sans-serif;\r\n      background: radial-gradient(circle at top left, #e0f4ff 0, #f7f9fc 40%, #ffffff 100%);\r\n      color: var(--text);\r\n      line-height: 1.6;\r\n      padding: 24px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    a {\r\n      color: var(--accent);\r\n      text-decoration: none;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .doc {\r\n      max-width: 960px;\r\n      margin: 0 auto 40px auto;\r\n      background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.98);\r\n      border-radius: 24px;\r\n      box-shadow: var(--shadow-soft);\r\n      overflow: hidden;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /* Header / Branding */\r\n    .doc-header {\r\n      background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--primary), #14213d);\r\n      color: #ffffff;\r\n      padding: 24px 32px;\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      align-items: center;\r\n      justify-content: space-between;\r\n      gap: 16px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-left {\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      flex-direction: column;\r\n      gap: 4px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 22px;\r\n      font-weight: 700;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.03em;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-tagline {\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.12em;\r\n      color: #e0fbfc;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .brand-pill {\r\n      padding: 6px 12px;\r\n      border-radius: 999px;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25);\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.12em;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /* Hero */\r\n    .hero {\r\n      padding: 32px;\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 3fr) minmax(0, 2fr);\r\n      gap: 24px;\r\n      align-items: center;\r\n      border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      background: linear-gradient(135deg, #ffffff, #e6f4ff);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 30px;\r\n      font-weight: 700;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-subtitle {\r\n      font-size: 15px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      margin-bottom: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-tags {\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      flex-wrap: wrap;\r\n      gap: 8px;\r\n      margin-bottom: 16px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .tag {\r\n      background: var(--accent-soft);\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n      padding: 6px 10px;\r\n      border-radius: 999px;\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.08em;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-meta {\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card {\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-lg);\r\n      padding: 18px 20px;\r\n      box-shadow: 0 12px 28px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.12);\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.35);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 16px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      margin-bottom: 10px;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card-list {\r\n      list-style: none;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .hero-card-list li {\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    /* Body */\r\n    .doc-body {\r\n      padding: 24px 32px 32px 32px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .section-title {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 20px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      margin-bottom: 8px;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .section-subtitle {\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      margin-bottom: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .contents-grid {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(190px, 1fr));\r\n      gap: 12px;\r\n      margin-bottom: 32px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-chip {\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-sm);\r\n      padding: 10px 12px;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      flex-direction: column;\r\n      gap: 3px;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-chip-title {\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-chip-meta {\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-section {\r\n      margin-bottom: 32px;\r\n      padding: 18px 18px 20px 18px;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-lg);\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.35);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-header {\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      align-items: center;\r\n      justify-content: space-between;\r\n      gap: 12px;\r\n      margin-bottom: 12px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-heading {\r\n      font-family: \"Poppins\", sans-serif;\r\n      font-size: 17px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-weight {\r\n      font-size: 11px;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.08em;\r\n      border-radius: 999px;\r\n      padding: 5px 10px;\r\n      background: var(--accent-soft);\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-body {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 3fr) minmax(0, 2fr);\r\n      gap: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-block-title {\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n      font-weight: 700;\r\n      text-transform: uppercase;\r\n      letter-spacing: 0.08em;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-list {\r\n      padding-left: 16px;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .unit-list li {\r\n      margin-bottom: 4px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .code-patterns {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: 1fr;\r\n      gap: 10px;\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .code-card {\r\n      background: #f8fafc;\r\n      border-radius: 12px;\r\n      padding: 10px 12px;\r\n      border: 1px dashed rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.6);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .code-card-title {\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n      font-weight: 600;\r\n      margin-bottom: 4px;\r\n      color: var(--primary);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    pre {\r\n      background: #0b1120;\r\n      color: #e2e8f0;\r\n      font-family: \"JetBrains Mono\", \"Fira Code\", monospace;\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n      border-radius: 10px;\r\n      padding: 10px 12px;\r\n      overflow-x: auto;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .two-column {\r\n      display: grid;\r\n      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.1fr) minmax(0, 1fr);\r\n      gap: 18px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .card-soft {\r\n      background: #ffffff;\r\n      border-radius: var(--radius-lg);\r\n      padding: 14px 16px;\r\n      border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .step-list,\r\n    .resource-list {\r\n      padding-left: 16px;\r\n      font-size: 13px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .step-list li,\r\n    .resource-list li {\r\n      margin-bottom: 6px;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    .doc-footer {\r\n      padding: 16px 32px 22px 32px;\r\n      border-top: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.3);\r\n      font-size: 12px;\r\n      color: var(--muted);\r\n      display: flex;\r\n      justify-content: space-between;\r\n      flex-wrap: wrap;\r\n      gap: 8px;\r\n      background: #f9fbff;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    @media (max-width: 768px) {\r\n      body {\r\n        padding: 12px;\r\n      }\r\n      .hero,\r\n      .unit-body,\r\n      .two-column {\r\n        grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr);\r\n      }\r\n      .doc-header {\r\n        flex-direction: column;\r\n        align-items: flex-start;\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  </style>\r\n</head>\r\n<body>\r\n\r\n<div class=\"doc\">\r\n\r\n  <header class=\"doc-header\">\r\n    <div class=\"brand-left\">\r\n      <div class=\"brand-title\">MyCollegeVerse.in</div>\r\n      <div class=\"brand-tagline\">Building Academic Identity · The Student OS</div>\r\n    </div>\r\n    <div class=\"brand-pill\">AKTU · B.Tech 1st Year · PPS (C)</div>\r\n  </header>\r\n\r\n  <section class=\"hero\">\r\n    <div>\r\n      <h1 class=\"hero-title\">Programming for Problem Solving – Smart Prep Doc</h1>\r\n      <p class=\"hero-subtitle\">\r\n        PPS (BCS101/BCS201) exam-oriented notes based on recent 5–6 years AKTU papers and latest important-topic lists.\r\n      </p>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"hero-tags\">\r\n        <span class=\"tag\">C Language</span>\r\n        <span class=\"tag\">PYQ Pattern</span>\r\n        <span class=\"tag\">Score 40+ / 50</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <p class=\"hero-meta\">\r\n        Start with loops and number patterns, then arrays and functions, and finally pointers, structures, DMA and file handling. \r\n        Ye doc tumhe direct scoring topics pe focus karne me help karega.\r\n      </p>\r\n    </div>\r\n\r\n    <aside class=\"hero-card\">\r\n      <div class=\"hero-card-title\">What You Get</div>\r\n      <ul class=\"hero-card-list\">\r\n        <li>• Unit-wise most repeated topics for PPS (C).</li>\r\n        <li>• List of must-do programs that appear again and again in AKTU exams.</li>\r\n        <li>• Smart steps to reach 40+ marks even with little time.</li>\r\n      </ul>\r\n    </aside>\r\n  </section>\r\n\r\n  <main class=\"doc-body\">\r\n\r\n    <section>\r\n      <h2 class=\"section-title\">Contents</h2>\r\n      <p class=\"section-subtitle\">\r\n        PPS units arranged by exam priority, not just syllabus order.\r\n      </p>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"contents-grid\">\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit 1 – Basics & Logic</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">Algorithms, flowcharts, languages, data types, operators.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit 2 – Control & Loops ★★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">if-else, switch, loops, classic number programs.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit 3 – Functions & Recursion ★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">Functions, parameter passing, recursion patterns.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit 4 – Arrays, Strings, Sorting ★★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">1D/2D arrays, strings, searching, sorting.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <div class=\"unit-chip\">\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-title\">Unit 5 – Pointers, Structures, DMA, Files ★★</span>\r\n          <span class=\"unit-chip-meta\">Pointer basics, structures, malloc/calloc, file I/O.</span>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit 2 – Control & Loops -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"unit2\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit 2 – Control Statements & Loops</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★★ · Most scoring</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Must-Know Concepts</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Decision statements: <code>if</code>, <code>if-else</code>, nested <code>if</code>, <code>switch</code> with menu-driven examples.</li>\r\n            <li>Loops: <code>for</code>, <code>while</code>, <code>do-while</code> – syntax, flow and small examples.</li>\r\n            <li>Jump statements: <code>break</code>, <code>continue</code>, <code>goto</code> – exam theory + short examples.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Most Repeated Programs</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Check whether a number is <strong>prime</strong>.</li>\r\n            <li>Check whether a number is <strong>palindrome</strong>.</li>\r\n            <li>Check whether a number is an <strong>Armstrong</strong> number.</li>\r\n            <li>Generate <strong>Fibonacci series</strong> up to n terms.</li>\r\n            <li>Find <strong>factorial</strong> of a number using loop.</li>\r\n            <li>Find <strong>sum of digits</strong> of a number.</li>\r\n            <li>Menu-driven <strong>simple calculator</strong> using <code>switch</code>.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Prime Number Pattern</div>\r\n            <pre>\r\nint n, i, flag = 0;\r\nprintf(\"Enter a number: \");\r\nscanf(\"%d\", &n);\r\nfor (i = 2; i <= n/2; i++) {\r\n    if (n % i == 0) {\r\n        flag = 1;\r\n        break;\r\n    }\r\n}\r\nif (flag == 0 && n > 1)\r\n    printf(\"Prime\");\r\nelse\r\n    printf(\"Not prime\");</pre>\r\n          </div>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Exam Hints</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Har program me clear input-output messages likho (user-friendly).</li>\r\n              <li>Loop ke andar sahi initialization/update check karo – off-by-one error se bach.</li>\r\n              <li>Comments se logic explain karo (2–3 lines max) for better presentation.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit 4 – Arrays & Strings -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"unit4\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit 4 – Arrays, Strings, Searching & Sorting</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★★ · Frequently asked</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Key Exam Topics</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>1D array: input, output, sum, average, largest and smallest element.</li>\r\n            <li>2D array (matrix): addition, subtraction and multiplication programs.</li>\r\n            <li>String handling: length, copy, compare, reverse using library and without library.</li>\r\n            <li>Searching: linear search and binary search in an array.</li>\r\n            <li>Sorting: bubble sort program (very high frequency), selection sort sometimes.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Standard Asked Programs</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Find largest element in an array.</li>\r\n            <li>Search an element using linear search / binary search.</li>\r\n            <li>Sort an array of numbers using bubble sort.</li>\r\n            <li>Multiply two matrices (check order before multiplication).</li>\r\n            <li>Reverse a string and check palindrome string.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Bubble Sort Pattern</div>\r\n            <pre>\r\nint a[50], n, i, j, temp;\r\nprintf(\"Enter n: \");\r\nscanf(\"%d\", &n);\r\nfor (i = 0; i < n; i++)\r\n    scanf(\"%d\", &a[i]);\r\n\r\nfor (i = 0; i < n-1; i++) {\r\n    for (j = 0; j < n-1-i; j++) {\r\n        if (a[j] > a[j+1]) {\r\n            temp = a[j];\r\n            a[j] = a[j+1];\r\n            a[j+1] = temp;\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n}\r\nprintf(\"Sorted array: \");\r\nfor (i = 0; i < n; i++)\r\n    printf(\"%d \", a[i]);</pre>\r\n          </div>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Matrix Multiplication Tips</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Check condition: columns of first matrix = rows of second matrix.</li>\r\n              <li>Use three nested loops: i (rows), j (columns), k (for multiplication and sum).</li>\r\n              <li>Initialize result matrix elements to 0 before inner loop.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit 3 – Functions & Recursion -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"unit3\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit 3 – Functions & Recursion</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Important Concepts</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Function definition, declaration and calling.</li>\r\n            <li>Return type, parameters, local and global variables.</li>\r\n            <li>Call by value vs call by reference (pointer use).</li>\r\n            <li>Recursion: idea, base case, recursive case.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Common Programs</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Factorial of a number using recursion.</li>\r\n            <li>Fibonacci series using recursion.</li>\r\n            <li>Sum of digits using recursion.</li>\r\n            <li>Swapping two numbers using call by reference (pointers).</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Factorial Using Recursion</div>\r\n            <pre>\r\nint fact(int n) {\r\n    if (n == 0 || n == 1)\r\n        return 1;\r\n    else\r\n        return n * fact(n - 1);\r\n}</pre>\r\n          </div>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Exam Hints</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Recursion me base case clearly likhna zaroori hai, warna infinite recursion ho sakti hai.</li>\r\n              <li>Call by reference wale program me address pass karo, pointer se value change karo.</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit 5 – Pointers, Structures, DMA, Files -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"unit5\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit 5 – Pointers, Structures, DMA & Files</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★★</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Key Theory Points</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Pointer definition, declaration, initialization, <code>*</code> and <code>&amp;</code> operators.</li>\r\n            <li>Pointers and arrays, pointer arithmetic basics.</li>\r\n            <li>Structures: definition, declaration, accessing members, array of structures.</li>\r\n            <li>Difference between structure and union.</li>\r\n            <li>Dynamic Memory Allocation: <code>malloc</code>, <code>calloc</code>, <code>realloc</code>, <code>free</code>.</li>\r\n            <li>File handling: <code>FILE*</code>, <code>fopen</code>, <code>fprintf</code>, <code>fscanf</code>, <code>fclose</code>.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">Typical Question Styles</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Explain pointers in C with examples. Why are they needed?</li>\r\n            <li>Write a program to store and display information of students using structure.</li>\r\n            <li>Write short notes on malloc, calloc, realloc and free.</li>\r\n            <li>Write a program to create a text file and write some content into it.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Simple File Write Pattern</div>\r\n            <pre>\r\nFILE *fp;\r\nfp = fopen(\"data.txt\", \"w\");\r\nif (fp == NULL) {\r\n    printf(\"File not created\");\r\n} else {\r\n    fprintf(fp, \"Hello PPS!\");\r\n    fclose(fp);\r\n}</pre>\r\n          </div>\r\n\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">DMA Pattern</div>\r\n            <pre>\r\nint *arr, n, i;\r\nprintf(\"Enter n: \");\r\nscanf(\"%d\", &n);\r\narr = (int *)malloc(n * sizeof(int));\r\nif (arr == NULL) {\r\n    printf(\"Memory not allocated\");\r\n} else {\r\n    for (i = 0; i < n; i++)\r\n        scanf(\"%d\", &arr[i]);\r\n}\r\nfree(arr);</pre>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Unit 1 – Basics -->\r\n    <section class=\"unit-section\" id=\"unit1\">\r\n      <div class=\"unit-header\">\r\n        <h3 class=\"unit-heading\">Unit 1 – Basics, Algorithms & Flowcharts</h3>\r\n        <span class=\"unit-weight\">Weightage: ★ (Easy theory)</span>\r\n      </div>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"unit-body\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <div class=\"unit-block-title\">Often Asked Theory</div>\r\n          <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n            <li>Definition of algorithm and flowchart; difference between them.</li>\r\n            <li>Advantages and limitations of flowcharts.</li>\r\n            <li>Types of programming languages: machine, assembly, high-level.</li>\r\n            <li>Compiler vs interpreter.</li>\r\n            <li>Data types in C and ranges; types of operators and precedence.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"code-patterns\">\r\n          <div class=\"code-card\">\r\n            <div class=\"code-card-title\">Quick Tip</div>\r\n            <ul class=\"unit-list\">\r\n              <li>Ye unit se short answers aate hain, jinhe rat ke 10–15 minute revision se pakka marks milte hain.</li>\r\n              <li>At least ek simple flowchart practice karo (sum of first n natural numbers jaisa).</li>\r\n            </ul>\r\n          </div>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n    <!-- Strategy -->\r\n    <section>\r\n      <h2 class=\"section-title\">Score 40+ / 50 Strategy</h2>\r\n      <p class=\"section-subtitle\">\r\n        Use this simple sequence if exam nazdeek hai.\r\n      </p>\r\n\r\n      <div class=\"two-column\">\r\n        <div class=\"card-soft\">\r\n          <h3 class=\"unit-block-title\">Priority Steps</h3>\r\n          <ol class=\"step-list\">\r\n            <li>Day 1–2: Master all loop-based programs (prime, palindrome, Armstrong, factorial, Fibonacci, sum of digits).</li>\r\n            <li>Day 3: Do arrays and sorting (largest in array, search, bubble sort, matrix multiplication).</li>\r\n            <li>Day 4: Functions and recursion (factorial, Fibonacci, sum using recursion; call by reference).</li>\r\n            <li>Day 5: Pointers, structures, DMA and simple file program.</li>\r\n            <li>Last day: Only theory revision (algorithm vs flowchart, data types, operators) + PYQ practice.</li>\r\n          </ol>\r\n        </div>\r\n\r\n        <div class=\"card-soft\">\r\n          <h3 class=\"unit-block-title\">MyCollegeVerse Resources</h3>\r\n          <ul class=\"resource-list\">\r\n            <li><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in/notes\" target=\"_blank\">MyCollegeVerse Notes</a> – more subject-wise notes and prep docs.</li>\r\n            <li><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in\" target=\"_blank\">MyCollegeVerse.in</a> – explore tools, notes and academic utilities.</li>\r\n            <li><a href=\"https://mycollegeverse.in/resume/build\" target=\"_blank\">Resume Builder</a> – use after 1st year to build your resume fast.</li>\r\n          </ul>\r\n        </div>\r\n      </div>\r\n    </section>\r\n\r\n  </main>\r\n\r\n  <footer class=\"doc-footer\">\r\n    <span>© MyCollegeVerse.in – The Student OS</span>\r\n    <span>Designed as a smart prep doc for AKTU B.Tech PPS (Programming for Problem Solving)</span>\r\n  </footer>\r\n\r\n</div>\r\n\r\n</body>\r\n</html>', 'pps-aktu-notes', NULL, NULL, 'PPS', 0, 9, 214, 0, '2026-05-13 15:33:56', '2026-05-13 15:33:56');

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `note_reviews`
--

CREATE TABLE `note_reviews` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `note_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `rating` tinyint(3) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `helped_in_exam` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `feedback` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `note_reviews`
--

INSERT INTO `note_reviews` (`id`, `user_id`, `note_id`, `rating`, `helped_in_exam`, `feedback`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(2, 4, 41, 5, 1, 'good', '2026-04-27 12:46:33', '2026-04-27 12:46:33'),
(3, 3, 55, 5, 1, 'high quality  and very precise notes for last  day preparation, Highly recommended', '2026-04-28 14:54:02', '2026-04-28 14:54:02');

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `notifications`
--

CREATE TABLE `notifications` (
  `id` char(36) NOT NULL,
  `type` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `notifiable_type` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `notifiable_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `data` text NOT NULL,
  `read_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- --------------------------------------------------------

--
-- Table structure for table `pages`
--

CREATE TABLE `pages` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `content` longtext DEFAULT NULL,
  `meta_description` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_active` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `pages`
--

INSERT INTO `pages` (`id`, `title`, `slug`, `content`, `meta_description`, `is_active`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(1, 'privacy policy', 'privacy-policy', '<h1>Privacy Policy</h1><h3>Your privacy matters to us. Here’s how we handle your data.</h3><p>At MyCollegeVerse, we respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal information.</p><p>This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your data when you use our platform.</p><h2>Information We Collect</h2><p>We may collect the following types of information:</p><p><b>Personal Information</b></p><p>Name Email address Contact details (if provided)</p><p><b>Usage Information</b></p><p>Pages visited Time spent on the platform Device and browser information</p><h2>How We Use Your Information</h2><p>We use your data to:</p><ul><li>Improve platform performance and user experience</li><li>Provide personalized content and recommendations</li><li>Communicate updates, features, or important notifications</li><li>Analyze user behavior to enhance our services</li></ul><h2>Data Sharing</h2><p>We do not sell your personal data.</p><p>We may share limited information with:</p><ul><li>Trusted service providers (for hosting, analytics, etc.)</li><li>Legal authorities if required by law</li></ul><h2>Cookies</h2><p>MyCollegeVerse may use cookies to enhance your experience, analyze traffic, and improve functionality.</p><p>You can disable cookies through your browser settings if you prefer.</p><h2>Data Security</h2><p>We take appropriate measures to protect your data from unauthorized access, misuse, or disclosure.</p><p>However, no online platform can guarantee complete security.</p><h2>Third-Party Links</h2><p>Our platform may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for their privacy practices or content.</p><h2>Your Rights</h2><p>You have the right to:</p><ul><li>Access your data</li><li>Request correction or deletion</li><li>Opt out of communications</li></ul><p>To exercise these rights, contact us at:<b>mycollegeverse@gmail.com</b></p><h2>Updates to This Policy</h2><p>We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page.</p><h2>Contact Us</h2><p>If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, you can contact us at:<b>mycollegeverse@gmail.com</b></p>', 'Privacy policy', 1, '2026-04-13 18:46:58', '2026-04-15 06:53:15'),
(2, 'Careers | MyCollegeVerse', 'careers', '<h1>Join Our Team</h1><h3>Help us build the future of student careers in India.</h3><p>At MyCollegeVerse, we are building more than just a platform — we are building a solution to one of the biggest problems students face: making the right career decisions.</p><p>We are a fast-growing, student-focused startup working at the intersection of education, technology, and career guidance. Our goal is to simplify how students discover colleges, explore careers, and plan their future. If you are passionate about building meaningful products and solving real-world problems, we would love to work with you.</p><h2>Why Work With Us</h2><ul><li>Work on real problems that impact students across India</li><li>Learn fast in a high-growth startup environment</li><li>Take ownership and build things that matter</li><li>Be part of a small, focused, and ambitious team</li><li>Opportunity to grow with the company</li></ul><h2>Open Roles</h2><p>Marketing & Growth Intern</p><h2>Who We Are Looking For</h2><p>We are not just looking for qualifications — we are looking for people who can think, build, and solve problems.</p><ul><li>Self-driven and curious</li><li>Willing to learn and experiment</li><li>Strong problem-solving mindset</li><li>Passionate about startups and impact</li></ul><h2>Apply</h2><p>Send your resume to:<b>mycollegeverse@gmail.com.</b>If you want to build something meaningful and grow fast, MyCollegeVerse is the place for you.</p>', NULL, 1, '2026-04-14 16:51:13', '2026-04-15 06:41:12'),
(3, 'Partner With MyCollegeVerse', 'partner', '<h1>Partner With Us</h1><h3>Let’s work together to empower students across India.</h3><p>MyCollegeVerse collaborates with colleges, educators, and organizations to create meaningful opportunities for students.</p><p>We believe that the right partnerships can significantly improve how students discover colleges, explore careers, and make decisions about their future.</p><p>Our platform connects you directly with a highly engaged student audience actively looking for guidance and opportunities.</p><h2>Who Can Partner With Us</h2><ul><li>Colleges and Universities</li><li>Coaching Institutes</li><li>EdTech Platforms</li><li>Career Experts and Mentors</li><li>Brands targeting students</li></ul><h2>Partnership Opportunities</h2><h3>College Partnerships</h3><p>Increase your institution’s visibility and reach students actively searching for colleges. Showcase your courses, campus, and key highlights.</p><h3>Brand Collaborations</h3><p>Promote your product or service to a targeted student audience through campaigns, featured placements, and content.</p><h3>Content Partnerships</h3><p>Co-create valuable content such as guides, webinars, and expert insights that help students make better decisions.</p><h3>Growth Partnerships</h3><p>Collaborate on campaigns, distribution strategies, and community building to expand reach and engagement.</p><h2>Why Partner With Us</h2><ul><li>Student-focused audience</li><li>Growing platform with strong engagement</li><li>Targeted reach for better conversions</li><li>Long-term partnership approach</li></ul><h2>Get Started</h2><p>Email us at:<b>mycollegeverse@gmail.com</b></p><p>Let’s build something impactful together and create better opportunities for students.</p>', 'Learn about MyCollegeVerse’s mission to simplify college decisions for students in India.', 1, '2026-04-14 16:51:13', '2026-04-15 06:43:30'),
(4, 'Help Center & FAQ', 'faq', '<h1>Help & FAQ</h1><h3>Find answers to common questions and get the help you need.</h3><p>Welcome to the MyCollegeVerse Help Center. We are here to make your experience smooth and hassle-free.</p><p>If you have questions about using the platform, exploring colleges, or understanding features, you’ll find answers below.</p><h2>General Questions</h2><p><b>What is MyCollegeVerse?</b></p><p>MyCollegeVerse is a student-focused platform that helps you discover colleges, explore career options, and make informed decisions about your future.</p><p><b>Who can use MyCollegeVerse?</b></p><p>Anyone can use the platform — students, parents, and educators looking for reliable college and career guidance.</p><p><b>Is MyCollegeVerse free to use?</b></p><p>Yes, most features on MyCollegeVerse are free for students. Some advanced tools or services may be introduced in the future.</p><h2>College & Career Guidance</h2><p><b>How do I choose the right college?</b></p><p>You can explore colleges on MyCollegeVerse using filters like course, location, and other factors. We aim to provide structured data to help you compare and decide easily.</p><p><b>Does MyCollegeVerse guarantee admission?</b></p><p>No, MyCollegeVerse does not guarantee admission. We provide information and tools to help you make better decisions.</p><p><b>Is the information on MyCollegeVerse accurate?</b></p><p>We strive to keep all information updated and accurate. However, students are advised to verify details from official college sources as well.</p><h2>Account & Usage</h2><p><b>Do I need to create an account?</b></p><p>You can browse most content without an account, but some features may require you to sign up.</p><p><b>How can I update my profile?</b></p><p>You can update your profile information from your dashboard after logging in.</p><p><b>I found incorrect information. What should I do?</b></p><p>Please report it to us at:<b>mycollegeverse@gmail.com</b>and our team will review and update it.</p><h2>Technical Support</h2><p><b>The website is not working properly. What should I do?</b></p><p>Try refreshing the page or clearing your browser cache. If the issue persists, contact us.</p><p><b>How can I contact support?</b></p><p>Email us at:<b>mycollegeverse@gmail.com</b></p><p>Still have questions? We’re here to help — reach out anytime and we’ll get back to you as soon as possible.</p>', '\"MyCollegeVerse Help & FAQ – Find answers about college selection, career guidance, platform usage, and student support.', 1, '2026-04-14 16:51:13', '2026-04-15 06:48:27'),
(5, 'About Us | mycollegevere', 'about-us', '<h1>About MyCollegeVerse</h1><h3>Helping students make smarter college and career decisions.</h3><p>MyCollegeVerse is a student-first platform built to simplify one of the most important decisions in a student’s life — choosing the right college and career path.</p><p>In today’s digital world, students are surrounded by information, but very little clarity. Most decisions are made based on incomplete data, peer pressure, or confusion.</p><p>We are here to change that.</p><h2>What is MyCollegeVerse?</h2><p>MyCollegeVerse is a platform where students can:</p><ul><li>Discover colleges easily</li><li>Explore career options</li><li>Compare choices with clarity</li><li>Make informed decisions</li></ul><p>We aim to replace confusion with clarity and guesswork with real insights.</p><h2>Why We Built This</h2><p>Choosing a college is not just about admission — it’s about your future.</p><p>We saw students struggling with:</p><ul><li>Too many options and no direction</li><li>Lack of reliable and structured information</li><li>No clear understanding of career outcomes</li></ul><p>This gap inspired us to build MyCollegeVerse — a platform designed to guide students at every step of their journey.</p><h2>Founder</h2><h3>Md Arsalan</h3><p>Md Arsalan is a B.Tech CSE student, SEO strategist, and frontend developer with hands-on experience in digital marketing and building scalable platforms.</p><p>He has worked on multiple projects and led growth and research initiatives, where he gained deep insights into how students search for information and make career decisions online.</p><h2>Founder’s Story</h2><p>“I’ve seen students make life-changing decisions without proper guidance. Most platforms either overload information or don’t provide clarity. MyCollegeVerse is built to simplify this journey — to give students the confidence to choose the right path.”</p><h2>What Makes Us Different</h2><ul><li>Student-first approach</li><li>Focus on clarity, not just information</li><li>Built with real student problems in mind</li><li>Continuous improvement based on user needs</li></ul><h2>Our Goal</h2><p>To become India’s most trusted platform for college discovery and career decision-making.</p><p>We are not just building a platform — we are building a solution to help students take control of their future.</p>\r\n<h2><b>Connect</b></h2><p>LinkedIn:<a href=\"https://in.linkedin.com/in/md-arsalan-web\"><b>Md Arsalan</b></a>\r\nTwitter/X:(optional)\r\nPortfolio:<a href=\"https://mdarsalan.vercel.app\"><b>Md Arsalan</b></a></p><p>We built MyCollegeVerse because we’ve experienced the confusion ourselves. And we are here to make sure the next generation doesn’t have to.</p>', 'Learn about MyCollegeVerse, a student-first platform helping students discover colleges, decisions with clarity and confidence.', 1, '2026-04-15 06:46:25', '2026-04-15 06:57:41'),
(6, 'Our Mission | MyCollegeVerse', 'our-mission', '<h1>Our Mission</h1><h3>To simplify every student’s college journey — from confusion to clarity.</h3><p>At MyCollegeVerse, our mission is to empower students with the clarity, tools, and insights they need to make the right college and career decisions.</p><p>We aim to eliminate confusion and replace it with structured, reliable, and easy-to-understand information.</p><h2>The Problem We Are Solving</h2><p>Millions of students struggle every year with:</p><ul><li>Choosing the right course or college</li><li>Understanding real career outcomes</li><li>Accessing trustworthy information</li><li>Making decisions without guidance</li></ul><p>This leads to poor choices, frustration, and lost opportunities.</p><h2>What We Aim To Achieve</h2><ul><li>Make college discovery simple and transparent</li><li>Provide clear and actionable career insights</li><li>Help students compare options effectively</li><li>Build tools that genuinely solve student problems</li></ul><h2>Our Vision</h2><p>We envision a future where every student in India can make confident, informed, and smart decisions about their education and career — without stress or confusion.</p><h2>Founder’s Vision</h2><p>Md Arsalan, Founder of MyCollegeVerse, built this platform with a clear purpose:</p><p>“I saw a major gap in how students make decisions. Most are confused, misinformed, or overwhelmed. My mission is to create a platform that gives them clarity, confidence, and the right direction.”</p><h2>Long-Term Vision</h2><ul><li>Become a one-stop platform for students</li><li>Integrate smart tools (AI, recommendations, planning tools)</li><li>Build a strong student ecosystem across India</li></ul><p>MyCollegeVerse exists to ensure that no student has to make life-changing decisions blindly.</p>', 'Mycollegeverse mission is to simplify every student’s college journey — from confusion to clarity', 1, '2026-04-15 06:58:50', '2026-04-15 06:58:50'),
(7, 'Terms & Conditions | MyCollegeVerse', 'terms-of-service', '<h1>Terms & Conditions</h1><h3>Please read these terms carefully before using MyCollegeVerse.</h3><p>Welcome to MyCollegeVerse. By accessing or using our platform, you agree to comply with and be bound by these Terms & Conditions.</p><p>If you do not agree with any part of these terms, please do not use our services.</p><h2>Use of Platform</h2><p>You agree to use MyCollegeVerse only for lawful purposes. You must not:</p><ul><li>Misuse the platform</li><li>Attempt unauthorized access</li><li>Use data for illegal or harmful activities</li></ul><h2>Information Accuracy</h2><p>We strive to provide accurate and updated information about colleges and careers. However, we do not guarantee the completeness or accuracy of all content.</p><p>Users are advised to verify important details from official sources.</p><h2>User Accounts</h2><p>If you create an account:</p><ul><li>You are responsible for maintaining confidentiality</li><li>You agree to provide accurate information</li><li>We reserve the right to suspend accounts if misuse is detected</li></ul><h2>Intellectual Property</h2><p>All content, design, and materials on MyCollegeVerse are the property of the platform and cannot be copied, reproduced, or distributed without permission.</p><h2>Third-Party Links</h2><p>Our platform may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for their content or practices.</p><h2>Limitation of Liability</h2><p>MyCollegeVerse is not liable for:</p><ul><li>Any decisions made based on platform content</li><li>Any loss or damage resulting from use of the platform</li></ul><h2>Changes to Terms</h2><p>We may update these Terms & Conditions at any time. Continued use of the platform means you accept the updated terms.</p><h2>Contact</h2><p>For any questions, contact:<b>mycollegeverse@gmail.com</b></p>', 'MyCollegeVerse Terms & Conditions to understand the rules, usage policies, and responsibilities while using our student-focused platform.', 1, '2026-04-15 07:02:01', '2026-04-15 07:02:28'),
(8, 'Cookies Policy', 'cookies-policy', '<h1>Cookie Policy</h1><h3>Understanding how we use cookies to improve your experience.</h3><h2>What Are Cookies?</h2><p>Cookies are small files stored on your device that help us improve website functionality and user experience.</p><h2>How We Use Cookies</h2><p>We use cookies to:</p><ul><li>Understand user behavior</li><li>Improve website performance</li><li>Remember user preferences</li></ul><h2>Types of Cookies</h2><ul><li>Essential Cookies (required for basic functionality)</li><li>Analytics Cookies (to track usage and performance)</li><li>Preference Cookies (to remember settings)</li></ul><h2>Managing Cookies</h2><p>You can disable cookies through your browser settings. However, some features of the platform may not work properly.</p><h2>Updates</h2><p>We may update this policy from time to time.</p>', 'Learn how MyCollegeVerse uses cookies to improve website performance, user experience, and personalization while ensuring transparency and control for users.', 1, '2026-04-15 07:05:27', '2026-04-15 07:05:27');

CREATE TABLE `password_resets` (

) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

-- --------------------------------------------------------

-- Table structure for table `personal_access_tokens`
--

CREATE TABLE `personal_access_tokens` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `tokenable_type` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `tokenable_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `token` varchar(64) NOT NULL,
  `abilities` text DEFAULT NULL,
  `last_used_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

CREATE TABLE `posts` (

  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `college_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `category` varchar(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'General',
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `slug` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `content` text NOT NULL,
  `image_path` varchar(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  `is_pinned` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--
-- Dumping data for table `posts`
--

INSERT INTO `posts` (`id`, `user_id`, `college_id`, `category`, `title`, `slug`, `content`, `image_path`, `is_pinned`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) VALUES
(2, 2, 5, 'Doubt', 'Docker - CSE', 'docker-cse-9Us3iC', 'how to use docker', NULL, 0, '2026-04-09 08:31:48', '2026-04-13 18:41:15'),
(17, 5, 5, 'Doubt', 'how to deploy node.js application', 'how-to-deploy-nodejs-application-uBzc9a67', 'is there any reliable and free services , really needed', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:38:25', '2026-04-14 20:38:25'),
(18, 3, 190, 'Career', 'Is B.Tech CSE still worth it in 2026? AI replacing coding jobs?', 'is-btech-cse-still-worth-it-in-2026-ai-replacing-ajIacQ8W', 'With tools like Cursor, Devin, and GPT-5 coming up, many students are scared. Is a traditional CSE degree still the best path, or should we focus exclusively on AI and prompt engineering? Let\'s discuss placements and future scope.', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(19, 3, 205, 'Career', 'CSE vs AIML vs Data Science: Which has the better salary in 2026?', 'cse-vs-aiml-vs-data-science-which-has-the-better-i0Pbm7sj', 'I am confused between these three branches. Older seniors say stick to core CSE, but new specializations are trending. Which one is getting recruited more currently in Tier-1 and Tier-2 colleges?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(20, 3, 200, 'Doubt', 'DSA vs Development: What should I focus on in 1st and 2nd year?', 'dsa-vs-development-what-should-i-focus-on-in-1st-knZLd3MB', 'Everyone says \'do DSA for FANG\', but startups want development projects. Is it possible to balance both? What is your daily schedule for 2025?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(21, 3, 149, 'General', 'Is Civil Engineering dead in India? Future with Smart Cities & AI.', 'is-civil-engineering-dead-in-india-future-with-sm-TBe0dB35', 'Core branches like Civil and Mechanical have seen low placements recently. But with the Smart Cities mission and AI in construction safety, is there a revival coming? Should I learn Revit or Python side-by-side?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(22, 3, 169, 'Career', 'MBBS ROI Reality Check: How many years to actually start earning?', 'mbbs-roi-reality-check-how-many-years-to-actually-P2Mu5bgf', 'Everyone sees the \'Doctor\' title, but nobody talks about the 10+ years of study + PG + residency. Is the financial ROI still there compared to an MBA or Tech career in 2026?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(23, 3, 165, 'Career', 'Can MBBS doctors switch to AI / Health-Tech?', 'can-mbbs-doctors-switch-to-ai-health-tech-vNfTteKX', 'I am a 3rd-year med student interested in technology. Are there genuine career paths where I can use my medical knowledge in the AI/Software space? Looking for advice on biomedical engineering or startups.', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(24, 3, 176, 'General', 'B.Com vs BBA vs CA: Best path for high-paying finance jobs.', 'bcom-vs-bba-vs-ca-best-path-for-high-paying-fina-QMhG7gQ3', 'Commerce students often get stuck in basic accounting roles. What are the modern skills (like FinTech or Data Analytics) that B.Com students must learn to compete with MBA grads in 2026?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(25, 3, 219, 'General', 'College Degree vs Skills: What matters more in the next 5 years?', 'college-degree-vs-skills-what-matters-more-in-the-qkB5T8ez', 'Elon Musk says degrees don\'t matter, but Indian HRs still ask for them. Is college just a waste of time for people who can learn everything online, or is the networking worth it?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(26, 3, 143, 'Career', 'How to earn money in college? Practical side hustles for students.', 'how-to-earn-money-in-college-practical-side-hustl-KxjugUKy', 'I want to be financially independent. Freelancing, content creation, or tutoring? What is working for you right now in 2025?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(27, 3, 158, 'Doubt', 'I wasted 3 years in college and have zero skills. What now?', 'i-wasted-3-years-in-college-and-have-zero-skills-MLz6Sdmx', 'Honestly, I just gamed and hung out. Now I\'m in my final year. Is it too late to restart? Give me a 6-month roadmap to get an entry-level job.', NULL, 0, '2026-04-14 20:43:54', '2026-04-14 20:43:54'),
(28, 5, 5, 'General', 'Need help!', 'need-help-WYlUq95u', 'Hey how to deploy nodejs project online?', NULL, 0, '2026-04-26 07:35:43', '2026-04-26 07:35:43'),
(29, 9, 214, 'Campus Life', 'AKTU News: Control Room Cameras Catch 160 Cheating Cases in Semester Exams', 'aktu-news-control-room-cameras-catch-160-cheating-VpzqRxLJ', 'this news appears to be genuine. The same report was published by Live Hindustan\r\n and mentions that during AKTU semester exams, more than 160 cheating cases were caught within two days through CCTV monitoring from a Lucknow control room', '/community_posts/post_1778442886_9_QC9Hf1F49', 0, '2026-05-10 19:54:48', '2026-05-10 19:54:48');

(2, 2, 'HC VERMA', 'Computer Science', 'IIT Delhi', 'HC VERMA SIR', '/requests/request_hc-verma_1776102882_LEGI-4SNZ.jpg', 'approved', '2026-04-13 17:54:44', '2026-04-13 18:24:07'),
(3, 2, 'hc verma new', 'iitkanpur', 'IIT Delhi', 'wow', '/requests/request_hc-verma-new_1776104691__HjrLqjzh.jpg', 'approved', '2026-04-13 18:24:53', '2026-04-13 18:25:02');

--
-- Table structure for table `projects`
--

CREATE TABLE `projects` (
  `id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `user_id` bigint(20) UNSIGNED NOT NULL,
  `title` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `description` text NOT NULL,
  `stream` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `file_url` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `cover_image_url` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
  `visibility_score` int(11) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `is_featured` tinyint(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  `metadata` longtext CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_bin DEFAULT NULL CHECK (json_valid(`metadata`)),
  `created_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL,
  `updated_at` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;

--

(1, 5, 'Test Manifest', 'testing how proof of work will look', 'Commerce', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1776497371/ynbyjtbtcidnbfskoojb.pdf', 'https://res.cloudinary.com/deqih9huc/image/upload/v1776497373/lavk3nbnwbkg3gcj5fqn.png', 10, 0, NULL, '2026-04-18 07:29:33', '2026-04-18 07:29:33');
